FILE: README.md
================================================
# Red Dog Mailer

AI-powered freight brokerage platform. Autonomous email processing, carrier scoring, rate negotiation, and load booking -- built for Red Dog Logistics.

## What It Does

An AI agent that connects to your Outlook inbox and handles the freight brokerage workflow end-to-end:

- **Syncs & classifies** emails into 6 categories: Tender, Shipper, Important, Shipper Status, Unimportant, Ignore
- **Extracts loads** from email bodies and PDF attachments (origin, destination, rate, weight, equipment, dates)
- **Three-path load matching** for carrier replies: order ID lookup, PO/reference number lookup, address+rate fuzzy matching
- **Scores carriers** on a 100-point scale using lane history, rating, insurance, and past response behavior
- **Generates personalized offer emails** to top carriers -- AI-written with dynamic pricing from learning loop
- **Negotiates rates** autonomously within configurable guardrails (ceiling %, max rounds)
- **Books loads** in TTMS when a carrier accepts (first-come-first-served race)
- **Learns from every outcome** -- negotiation patterns feed back into future AI prompts
- **Proactively finds unbooked loads** in TTMS and blasts carriers without waiting for emails
- **Pod-scoped filtering** -- users only see loads belonging to their TTMS pod
- **Sent mail tracking** -- syncs and displays outbound emails from Outlook
- **Manual reply with AI Draft** -- one-click AI-generated generic replies for any email

## Architecture

```
Outlook (Graph API) --> Email Sync --> AI Classification --> Pipeline Routing
                                                                 |
                     +-------------------------------------------+--------------------+-----------------+
                     |                    |                       |                    |                 |
                  TENDER              SHIPPER               IMPORTANT          SHIPPER_STATUS        UNIMP
                Extract lanes       Extract loads         3-path matching      Lookup load by       Auto-mark
                Generate quotes     Create TTMS order     1. Order ID          reference number     processed
                Send CSV reply      Blast carriers        2. PO/Ref number     Draft status reply
                     |                    |               3. Address+rate            |
                Track bidding      Score -> Offer              |              Send reply
                Await award        -> Send -> Track       Negotiate rate
                     |                    |                    |
                Convert to order  Race: first accept     Accept/Counter/
                on award          wins, cancel rest      Walk away
                                        |                    |
                                  Learning loop        Learning loop
                                  captures outcome     captures outcome
```

## Email Classification

| Category | Description | Action |
|----------|-------------|--------|
| **TENDER** | RFPs, bid requests, forwarded load lists, rate sheets | Extract lanes, generate quotes, send CSV |
| **SHIPPER** | Direct load awards from shippers | Extract load, create TTMS order, blast carriers |
| **IMP** | Carrier replies, rate quotes, booking confirmations, status updates | Match load, negotiate or draft reply |
| **SHIPPER_STATUS** | Shipper asking "where is my load?" | Look up load, draft status reply |
| **UNIMP** | FYI notifications, TMS dispatch alerts, newsletters | Auto-mark processed, no action |
| **IGNORE** | Spam, marketing, OOO, automated junk | Auto-mark processed |

### IMP Load Matching (3 paths)

When a carrier emails about a load, the system tries to match it in order:

1. **Order ID** -- "order 134637", "ORD-134637", "load #588" --> direct TTMS order lookup
2. **PO/Reference number** -- "PO 12345", "ref #7890" --> TTMS `?reference_number=` API search
3. **Address + rate** -- "Dallas TX to Chicago" + "$1500" --> `searchOrdersByAddress` with closest-rate matching

If a specific order number was extracted but not found, the system stops -- it won't fall through to fuzzy matching and accidentally grab the wrong order.

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Framework | Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack) |
| Database | Supabase (PostgreSQL + Realtime + RLS) |
| AI | OpenRouter (Gemini 3 Flash) |
| Email | Microsoft Graph API |
| TMS | Red Dog TTMS API |
| Voice | VAPI (call transcript extraction) |
| Deployment | Vercel (serverless + cron) |
| UI | Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Recharts |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- Node.js 18+
- Supabase project
- Microsoft Azure AD app (for Outlook OAuth)
- OpenRouter API key
- TTMS API access

### Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Eeman1113/ttms_mailer.git
cd ttms_mailer
npm install
cp .env.example .env.local
# Fill in your values in .env.local
# Apply all SQL files in supabase/migrations/ to your Supabase project
npm run dev
```

### Environment Variables

See [.env.example](.env.example) for all required variables:

- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` -- Supabase
- `AZURE_AD_CLIENT_ID` / `AZURE_AD_CLIENT_SECRET` / `AZURE_AD_TENANT_ID` -- Microsoft OAuth
- `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` -- AI models
- `REDDOG_TTMS_API_KEY` -- TMS integration
- `CRON_SECRET` -- Vercel cron auth
- `VAPI_API_KEY` -- Voice call transcription

## Agent Modes

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| **Off** | Email sync only, no processing |
| **Test** | Full pipeline runs, nothing sent -- drafts created with `is_test=true` for review |
| **Live** | Fully autonomous -- negotiation replies instant, other emails gated by draft mode |

## Key Features

### Carrier Scoring
100-point scale: lane history (35pts), rating (25pts), approval (15pts), insurance (15pts), active status (10pts). Response-history bonus: +15 for reliable responders, -15 for serial ghosters.

### Dynamic Pricing
LLM decides offer rates using: current DAT (with fuel surcharge), old DAT, carrier's last rate, shipper rate, learning loop data. Hard 10% margin floor enforced server-side. Falls back to formula if LLM fails.

### Safety Rails
Rate ceiling enforcement, max negotiation rounds, low-confidence escalation, keyword detection (legal threats), circuit breaker (3+ escalations/hour auto-pauses agent). Wrong-order guard prevents matching unrelated loads when a specific order number lookup fails.

### Learning Loop
Every negotiation event captured with full context. Two levels:
- **L1**: Carrier blast history (offered $X --> accepted/expired/countered)
- **L2**: Lane intelligence (acceptance rates, avg closing rates on corridor)

Patterns distilled nightly and injected into AI prompts.

### Proactive Reblast
Scans TTMS for PENDING orders with no carrier. Re-blasts loads where all previous carriers expired/declined. Excludes already-contacted carriers.

### Tender Quoting
Extracts lanes from RFP emails + PDFs, generates AI rate quotes using lane boundaries, sends CSV reply, tracks bidding through award.

### Pod-Scoped Filtering
Users with a TTMS pod assignment only see loads belonging to their pod. Implemented via `in_user_pod` column on `quote_board_loads`, updated on every sync/refresh cycle via TTMS pod API.

### Manual Reply with AI Draft
One-click AI-generated reply for any email via `/api/emails/draft-generic-reply`. Reads email context (subject, body, category, intent) and writes a natural human response -- no load matching or rate quoting. User can edit before sending.

### Sent Mail
Syncs outbound emails from Outlook via Graph API. Separate sent mail view in the sidebar.

### Admin Panel
User management, analytics overview, and system monitoring at `/admin`.

## Project Structure

```
src/
  app/
    (dashboard)/              # UI pages
      inbox/                  # Email inbox with filters
      email/[id]/             # Email detail + reply + load extraction
      sent/                   # Sent mail view
      dashboard/              # Agent control panel
      analytics/              # Email & performance analytics
      loads/                  # Load management + create
      carriers/               # Carrier directory
      shippers/               # Shipper management
      quote-board/            # Quote board with pod scoping
      escalations/            # Escalation queue
      lane-boundaries/        # Lane rate boundaries
      outbound/               # Outbound blast management
      settings/               # User settings
      agent/                  # Agent run/stop controls
    admin/                    # Admin panel
    api/                      # 50+ API routes
      agent/                  # Agent control (run, settings, drafts, stop)
      emails/                 # Email ops (draft-reply, draft-generic-reply, extract, send, sync-sent)
      cron/                   # 11 scheduled jobs
      quote-board/            # Quote board CRUD + sync + backfill
      admin/                  # Admin endpoints (users, analytics, overview)
      loads/                  # Load CRUD
      carriers/               # Carrier lookup
      shippers/               # Shipper management
  lib/
    agent/                    # Agent system
      orchestrator.ts         # Main cycle: sync -> classify -> route -> process
      carrier-blast.ts        # Carrier outreach + reblast
      carrier-reply-handler.ts # Negotiation pipeline
      race-manager.ts         # First-accept-wins booking
      tender-pipeline.ts      # Tender/RFP processing
      shipper-pipeline.ts     # Shipper load processing
      shipper-status-handler.ts # Status inquiry replies
      extract-loads.ts        # AI load extraction from emails + PDFs
      draft-manager.ts        # Send vs draft routing
      safety.ts               # Escalation + circuit breaker
      activity-logger.ts      # Agent activity logging
      settings-cache.ts       # Cached agent settings
    ai/                       # LLM layer
      classify.ts             # Email classification (6 categories, 23 intents)
      prompts.ts              # All prompt templates
      draft.ts                # AI reply drafting
      openrouter-client.ts    # OpenRouter API client with retry
      extract-vapi.ts         # VAPI transcript extraction
    negotiate.ts              # Negotiation engine (intent parsing, rate calc, counter generation)
    carrier-scoring.ts        # 100-point scoring + offer email generation
    learning/                 # Negotiation learning loop
    graph-client.ts           # Microsoft Graph API (email, calendar, send)
    graph/subscription.ts     # Webhook subscription management
    email-sync.ts             # Email sync + classification pipeline
    ttms/
      client.ts               # TTMS API (orders, carriers, clients, address search)
      get-ttms-user-id.ts     # Pod info resolution + caching
      pod-load-ids.ts         # Pod-scoped load ID fetching
      order-cache.ts          # Order cache layer
    quote-board/
      refresh.ts              # Full quote board refresh with pod scoping
      incremental.ts          # Incremental quote materialization
  components/
    email-detail/             # Email view (auto-reply, manual-reply, conversation thread)
    inbox/                    # Email list, filters, category badges
    dashboard/                # Quote board tab, call log tab
    shell/                    # Layout (LeftNav, TopBar, PodWatcher)
    ui/                       # shadcn components
  types/
    index.ts                  # Core types (ClassificationResult, 23 intents, etc.)
    database.ts               # Supabase database types
    email.ts                  # Email-specific types
supabase/
  migrations/                 # 26 SQL migrations
```

## Cron Jobs

| Job | Schedule | Purpose |
|-----|----------|---------|
| Agent cycle | Daily 9pm | Process emails for all enabled users |
| Delta sync | Midnight | Full email sync |
| Token refresh | 6am | Refresh OAuth tokens |
| Graph renew | Noon | Renew webhook subscriptions |
| TTMS sync | 6pm | Sync carriers, clients, orders |
| Pattern distill | 3am | Distill negotiation learnings |
| Ghost detect | 9am | Flag ghosted negotiations |
| VAPI extract | 3pm | Process call transcripts |
| Quote board refresh | Every 4h | Refresh quote board data |
| Check escalations | Every 2h | Review pending escalations |
| Sync pods | Daily 7am | Sync pod assignments from TTMS |

## Cost

~$0.01 per agent cycle. ~$7/month running 24/7.

-- WARNING: This schema is for context only and is not meant to be run.
-- Table order and constraints may not be valid for execution.

CREATE TABLE public.accounts (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id uuid NOT NULL,
  provider text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'azure'::text,
  access_token text,
  refresh_token text,
  expires_at bigint,
  created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT accounts_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT accounts_user_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.ai_activity_log (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  action_type text NOT NULL,
  action_description text NOT NULL,
  load_id uuid,
  carrier_id uuid,
  shipper_id uuid,
  email_id uuid,
  external_id text,
  outcome text,
  metadata jsonb,
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT ai_activity_log_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT ai_activity_log_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id),
  CONSTRAINT ai_activity_log_load_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (load_id) REFERENCES public.loads(id),
  CONSTRAINT ai_activity_log_carrier_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (carrier_id) REFERENCES public.carriers(id),
  CONSTRAINT ai_activity_log_shipper_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (shipper_id) REFERENCES public.shippers(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.carrier_quotes (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  load_id uuid NOT NULL,
  carrier_id uuid NOT NULL,
  quoted_rate numeric,
  rate_per_mile numeric,
  status text DEFAULT 'pending'::text,
  ai_counter_rate numeric,
  email_thread_id text,
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  final_rate numeric,
  outcome text,
  outcome_at timestamp with time zone,
  booked_at timestamp with time zone,
  rounds_to_close integer,
  CONSTRAINT carrier_quotes_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT carrier_quotes_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id),
  CONSTRAINT carrier_quotes_load_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (load_id) REFERENCES public.loads(id),
  CONSTRAINT carrier_quotes_carrier_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (carrier_id) REFERENCES public.carriers(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.carriers (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  name text NOT NULL,
  email text,
  phone text,
  mc_number text,
  dot_number text,
  equipment_types ARRAY DEFAULT '{}'::text[],
  preferred_lanes jsonb DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,
  relationship_notes text,
  contact_name text,
  status boolean DEFAULT true,
  approved boolean DEFAULT false,
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT carriers_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT carriers_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.email_connections (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  provider text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'microsoft'::text,
  email_address text NOT NULL,
  access_token text NOT NULL,
  refresh_token text NOT NULL,
  token_expires_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL,
  graph_subscription_id text,
  graph_subscription_expires_at timestamp with time zone,
  delta_link text,
  last_synced_at timestamp with time zone,
  is_active boolean DEFAULT true,
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT email_connections_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT email_connections_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.email_negotiations (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

  sender_email text NOT NULL,
  sender_name text,
  mc_number text DEFAULT 'NA'::text,
  interested_order text NOT NULL,
  last_negotiated_rate numeric,
  intent text,
  conversation_id text,
  status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open'::text CHECK (status = ANY (ARRAY['open'::text, 'negotiating'::text, 'accepted'::text, 'rejected'::text, 'closed'::text])),
  round_count integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  first_contact_at timestamp with time zone,
  closed_at timestamp with time zone,
  final_rate numeric,
  outcome text,
  CONSTRAINT email_negotiations_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT email_negotiations_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES auth.users(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.email_replies (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

  CONSTRAINT email_replies_user_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id),
  CONSTRAINT email_replies_email_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (email_id) REFERENCES public.emails(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.emails (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id uuid NOT NULL,
  graph_message_id text NOT NULL,
  subject text,
  from_address text NOT NULL,
  from_name text,
  to_addresses ARRAY DEFAULT '{}'::text[],
  received_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL,
  body_preview text,
  body_html text,
  is_read boolean DEFAULT false,
  has_attachments boolean DEFAULT false,
  conversation_id text,
  category USER-DEFINED DEFAULT 'UNCLASSIFIED'::email_category,
  ai_reasoning text,
  confidence real,
  manual_override boolean DEFAULT false,
  created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  detected_intent text,
  confidence_score numeric,
  classifier_reasoning text,
  extracted_fields jsonb,
  agent_corrected_intent text,
  handler_status text DEFAULT 'pending'::text,
  handler_triggered text,
  handler_result jsonb,
  load_id uuid,
  carrier_id uuid,
  shipper_id uuid,
  escalation_id uuid,
  CONSTRAINT emails_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT emails_user_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id),
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_load FOREIGN KEY (load_id) REFERENCES public.loads(id),
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_carrier FOREIGN KEY (carrier_id) REFERENCES public.carriers(id),
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_shipper FOREIGN KEY (shipper_id) REFERENCES public.shippers(id),
  CONSTRAINT fk_emails_escalation FOREIGN KEY (escalation_id) REFERENCES public.escalations(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.escalations (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  load_id uuid,
  trigger_type text NOT NULL,
  status text DEFAULT 'open'::text,

  options jsonb NOT NULL,
  ai_recommendation text,
  resolved_option text,
  resolved_at timestamp with time zone,
  resolution_source text,
  notification_email_sent boolean DEFAULT false,
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT escalations_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT escalations_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id),
  CONSTRAINT escalations_load_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (load_id) REFERENCES public.loads(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.lane_boundaries (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  lane_description text NOT NULL,
  origin_state text NOT NULL,
  dest_state text NOT NULL,
  equipment_type text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Dry Van'::text,

  target_rate numeric NOT NULL,
  ceiling_rate numeric NOT NULL,
  created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  set_by text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'agent'::text,
  CONSTRAINT lane_boundaries_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT lane_boundaries_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES auth.users(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.learnings (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  scope_type text NOT NULL,
  carrier_email text,
  carrier_name text,
  carrier_mc text,
  origin_state text,
  dest_state text,
  equipment_type text,

  observation text NOT NULL,

  sample_size integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  structured_data jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
  status text DEFAULT 'active'::text CHECK (status = ANY (ARRAY['active'::text, 'superseded'::text, 'invalidated'::text])),
  superseded_by uuid,
  source_event_ids ARRAY DEFAULT '{}'::uuid[],
  extracted_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  expires_at timestamp with time zone,
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),

  CONSTRAINT learnings_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id),
  CONSTRAINT learnings_superseded_by_fkey FOREIGN KEY (superseded_by) REFERENCES public.learnings(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.loads (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

  load_number text NOT NULL,
  status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'::text,

  equipment_type text,

  delivery_appointment timestamp with time zone,
  carrier_rate numeric,
  shipper_rate numeric,

  miles numeric,

  CONSTRAINT loads_carrier_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (carrier_id) REFERENCES public.carriers(id)
);

  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

  carrier_email text,

  carrier_mc text,
  carrier_id uuid,

  load_number text,
  email_id uuid,
  negotiation_id uuid,

  dest_city text,
  dest_state text,
  equipment_type text,
  shipper_rate numeric,
  carrier_offer numeric,
  ai_counter_rate numeric,
  floor_rate numeric,
  target_rate numeric,
  ceiling_rate numeric,
  round_number integer,

  model_used text,
  system_prompt_hash text,
  action_taken text,

  CONSTRAINT negotiation_events_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id),
  CONSTRAINT negotiation_events_carrier_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (carrier_id) REFERENCES public.carriers(id),
  CONSTRAINT negotiation_events_load_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (load_id) REFERENCES public.loads(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.profiles (
  id uuid NOT NULL,
  email text,
  name text,
  avatar_url text,

);
CREATE TABLE public.quote_board_loads (

);
CREATE TABLE public.quote_board_quotes (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  load_id text NOT NULL,
  source text NOT NULL,
  identifier text NOT NULL,
  display_name text,
  latest_rate numeric,
  status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open'::text,
  round_count integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  is_known boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  carrier_id text,
  mc_number text,
  last_activity timestamp with time zone,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  reference_id text,
  CONSTRAINT quote_board_quotes_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT quote_board_quotes_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.rate_parameters (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  lane_description text,
  origin_state text,
  dest_state text,
  equipment_type text,
  floor_rate numeric,
  target_rate numeric,
  ceiling_rate numeric,
  is_default boolean DEFAULT false,
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT rate_parameters_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT rate_parameters_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.shippers (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  name text NOT NULL,
  billing_email text,
  logistics_email text,
  sla_update_interval_hours integer DEFAULT 4,
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT shippers_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT shippers_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.sync_state (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id uuid NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  last_sync_at timestamp with time zone,
  delta_link text,
  sync_in_progress boolean DEFAULT false,
  created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT sync_state_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT sync_state_user_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.user_preferences (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id uuid NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  auto_classify boolean DEFAULT true,
  important_keywords ARRAY DEFAULT '{}'::text[],
  ignore_domains ARRAY DEFAULT '{}'::text[],
  sync_interval_minutes integer DEFAULT 15,
  created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT user_preferences_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT user_preferences_user_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);
CREATE TABLE public.vapi_call_extractions (
  id uuid NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  call_id text NOT NULL,
  phone_number text,
  order_id text,
  rate numeric,
  intent text DEFAULT 'unknown'::text,
  status text DEFAULT 'extracted'::text,
  call_started_at timestamp with time zone,
  summary text,
  extracted_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
  CONSTRAINT vapi_call_extractions_pkey PRIMARY KEY (id),
  CONSTRAINT vapi_call_extractions_tenant_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (tenant_id) REFERENCES public.profiles(id)
);


================================================

FILE: FORTRESS_ISSUE_STATUS.md

# FORTRESS SYSTEM — Issue & Fix Status



## Detailed Status

### CRITICAL Issues (4)

#### F-001 | Cannot reply to or compose emails from inside Fortress
- **Priority:** CRITICAL
- **Status:** Fixed
- **Notes:** A compose page exists at `/inbox/compose` for new emails. Inline reply from within the email detail view is implimented and can be used

---

#### F-002 | Active loads not filtered to assigned Pod — showing all company loads
- **Priority:** CRITICAL
- **Status:** Fixed
- **Fix Date:** April 13–14, 2026
- **What was done:**
  - Switched all TTMS GET APIs from `user_id` filtering to `pod_id` filtering — this is the correct scoping mechanism in TTMS
  - Added `getTtmsPodInfo()` helper that resolves a user's pod from their TTMS profile
  - Updated all data-fetching routes: loads, shippers, analytics, quote board, outbound, draft-reply
  - Quote board uses `in_user_pod` tagging to scope loads per pod
  - Added automated pod change detection — cron checks TTMS every 10 minutes, updates Supabase, and the browser shows a toast notification prompting the user to refresh when their pod assignment changes

---

#### F-003 | AI offer price being recorded as the carrier's price — completely incorrect data
- **Priority:** CRITICAL
- **Status:** Fixed
- **Fix Date:** April 14, 2026
- **What was done:**
  - Added a new **AI Rate** column to the Quote Board alongside the existing **Carrier Offer** column — these are now two separate data points
  - Rewrote the VAPI call extraction prompt (v4) with explicit rules distinguishing the human carrier's rate from the AI's offered rate
  - Added concrete examples to the prompt (e.g., "AI offered $6000, user requested $8000" → carrier rate = 8000, ai_rate = 6000)
  - If the carrier never stated a rate (declined, hung up, asked to be transferred), the carrier offer shows "Pending" instead of the AI's number
  - Added `ai_rate` column to both `vapi_call_extractions` and `quote_board_quotes` database tables
  - Wired `ai_rate` through the full pipeline: extraction → sync → materialization → API → frontend
  - Re-extracted all 100 existing VAPI calls with the corrected prompt and updated all 6 tenants
  - Quote board detail page now shows: Carrier Offer (green), AI Rate (orange), Client Rate (blue)

---

#### F-004 | Load marked as booked but call was forwarded to agent — status conflict
- **Priority:** CRITICAL
- **Status:** Fixed
- **Fix Date:** April 14, 2026
- **What was done:**
  - VAPI calls with `intent: "booking"` now map to status **"negotiating"** instead of "accepted" — a verbal agreement on a phone call is not a confirmed booking
  - VAPI calls with `intent: "transfer"` map to status **"transferred"** — clearly flagged for human review
  - The **"Accepted" / "Best Book"** status on the quote board can now only be set through the email negotiation flow where an MC number is confirmed
  - No phone call alone can mark a load as booked — prevents the forwarded-call-marked-as-booked scenario

---

### IMPORTANT Issues (5)

#### F-005 | Email categories are incorrectly defined — terminology does not match freight industry
- **Priority:** IMPORTANT
- **Status:** Not Started
- **Notes:** Current categories (Shipper, Carrier, etc.) need to be remapped to freight-standard terms: Tender, Rate Request, Status Inquiry, Load Blaster, Bidding, Unimportant. Requires updating the AI classification prompt and the inbox filter UI.

---

#### F-006 | Location disambiguation shows irrelevant addresses — no way to view PDF to verify
- **Priority:** IMPORTANT
- **Status:** Fixed
- **Fix Date:** April 14, 2026
- **What was done:**
  - Implemented **name-first customer matching** — the system now extracts facility names from carrier notification PDFs (e.g., "IKO Southwest Inc. - Hillsboro", "QXO-QXO-Kansas City KS") and matches them against customer names before falling back to city/state matching
  - Added `matchCustomerByName()` function using fuzzy matching (exact → contains → word match)
  - This eliminates most irrelevant results (e.g., Nicoma Park Lumber no longer appears when matching an IKO shipment)


---

#### F-007 | Default client selection picks wrong client
- **Priority:** IMPORTANT
- **Status:** Fixed
- **Fix Date:** April 14, 2026
- **What was done:**
  - Existing `matchShipper()` fuzzy matching already matches client names from email subjects/signatures (exact → contains → word match, strips LLC/Inc/Corp suffixes)
  - Today's name-first matching improvement helps when carrier notification PDFs contain the client name (e.g., "Carrier Must bill: IKO Industries Inc.")
  - Auto-populates bill type, BOL number, and reference numbers from PDFs which reduces manual entry errors
  - Email domain-based matching (e.g., `@iko.com` → IKO Industries) 

---

#### F-008 | Outbound carrier lanes not filtered to Pod 2 — showing all company lanes
- **Priority:** IMPORTANT
- **Status:** Fixed
- **Fix Date:** April 13, 2026
- **What was done:**
  - All TTMS data-fetching routes switched from `user_id` to `pod_id` filtering, including the outbound/carrier blast flow
  - Outbound lane view now only shows lanes belonging to the logged-in user's pod

---

#### F-009 | Call log shows all users' calls — not filtered to user's loads
- **Priority:** IMPORTANT
- **Status:** Started and done according to la
- **Notes:** The Call Log tab currently displays all VAPI calls across all users/pods. Needs pod-scoped filtering similar to what was done for Active Loads and Quote Board.

---

### NICE TO HAVE Issues (3)

#### F-010 | Unimportant classification logic is unknown and uncontrollable
- **Priority:** NICE TO HAVE
- **Status:** Not Started
- **Notes:** Classificati
- on rules are embedded in the AI prompt. Need to document them, expose to users, and add reclassification feedback.

---

#### F-011 | Rate sourcing for AI-generated quotes is not transparent
- **Priority:** NICE TO HAVE
- **Status:** Not Started
- **Notes:** The AI pricing engine uses DAT market rates, carrier history, lane intelligence, and a 10% margin floor — but this breakdown is not shown to the user. The AI reasoning is logged but not displayed in the quote board.

---

## Additional Fixes Deployed 

These were not in the original issue list but were identified and fixed during today's session:

| Fix | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| **Quote board PENDING filter** | Quote board defaults to showing only PENDING loads. Non-pending loads (Picked Up, Delivered, etc.) are filtered via frontend pills with a collapsible expand arrow. |
| **Order status from TTMS** | Each quote board load now shows its TTMS order status (Pending, Awaiting Pickup, Picked Up, Delivered, Carrier Check, Paid) as a color-coded badge. Auto-syncs via cron. |
| **Shipper rate backfill** | Loads with missing client rate (`shipper_rate`) now auto-populate from the TTMS `total_charge` field on sync. |
| **PDF data extraction** | Carrier notification PDFs now auto-extract: facility names (Ship From/To), bill type (Prepaid/Collect), BOL number, reference numbers (Order #, Customer PO #, IKO Reference), equipment type, and scheduled dates. |
| **VAPI cron optimization** | VAPI extraction cron optimized from ~60s to ~14s — fetches calls once, extracts once, distributes to all tenants. No longer times out on cron-job.org. |
| **Live activity feed** | VAPI sync results now appear in the Live Activity feed in real-time. |
| **Pod change detection** | New cron detects pod assignment changes in TTMS every 10 minutes. Browser shows a toast notification when a user's pod changes, prompting them to refresh. |
| **PRO # regex fix** | Fixed a bug where "PROPERTY" in carrier instructions was being extracted as a PRO # reference number. |



================================================
FILE: GETTING_STARTED.md
================================================
# TTMS Mailer — Getting Started Guide

A step-by-step walkthrough for setting up your account, understanding the platform, and using every feature.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Account Setup (Admin Side)](#1-account-setup-admin-side)
2. [First Login](#2-first-login)
3. [Understanding Your Dashboard](#3-understanding-your-dashboard)
4. [Inbox & Email Management](#4-inbox--email-management)
5. [Working with Loads](#5-working-with-loads)
6. [Carrier Network](#6-carrier-network)
7. [Shipper Accounts](#7-shipper-accounts)
8. [Quote Board](#8-quote-board)
9. [Outbound — Carrier Offers](#9-outbound--carrier-offers)
10. [Call Log (VAPI)](#10-call-log-vapi)
11. [Lane Boundaries](#11-lane-boundaries)
12. [Escalations](#12-escalations)
13. [Agent Mode (Autopilot)](#13-agent-mode-autopilot)
14. [Analytics](#14-analytics)
15. [Settings](#15-settings)
16. [Pod Scoping — How It Works](#16-pod-scoping--how-it-works)
17. [Admin Dashboard](#17-admin-dashboard)
18. [Common Workflows](#18-common-workflows)

---

## 1. Account Setup (Admin Side)

Before a user can log in, their account needs to be configured in RedDog TTMS.

### Step 1: Create a TTMS User

1. Go to the RedDog TTMS admin panel
2. Create a new user with the person's **Microsoft Outlook email address**
3. This email must match exactly — the system uses it to link TTMS and Outlook accounts

### Step 2: Assign the User to a Pod

1. In TTMS, assign the user to the appropriate **Pod** (e.g., "Agent" pod)
2. The pod determines which loads, shippers, and clients the user can see
3. If the user should see all loads (no restrictions), do NOT assign them to a pod

### Step 3: Add Clients and Shippers to the Pod

1. In TTMS, add the relevant **clients (shippers)** to the user's pod
2. Add the relevant **carriers** if your pod structure uses carrier assignments
3. Any loads created by this user will automatically be scoped to their pod

### Step 4: Verify Setup

Make sure:
- The user has a valid Microsoft 365 / Outlook email
- The user is listed in TTMS with that exact email
- The user's pod has clients/shippers assigned to it (if applicable)

---

## 2. First Login

### Step 1: Go to the App

Open the TTMS Mailer URL in your browser (e.g., `https://mailer-five-lemon.vercel.app`)

### Step 2: Sign in with Microsoft

1. Click **"Sign in with Microsoft Outlook"**
2. You'll be redirected to Microsoft's login page
3. Enter your Outlook email and password
4. Grant the requested permissions (email read/write, profile)

### Step 3: Automatic Setup

On first login, the system automatically:
- Looks up your email in TTMS to find your user record
- Detects your **pod** (if assigned) and saves it to your profile
- Stores your **TTMS user ID** for API filtering
- Connects your Outlook inbox for email sync
- Marks your onboarding as complete
- Redirects you to the Dashboard

### What If You're Not in TTMS?

You can still log in. You'll have access to all features, but:
- Loads won't be filtered by pod (you'll see everything)
- Creating loads/shippers won't be scoped to a specific pod
- Your admin can add you to TTMS later — next login will pick it up

---

## 3. Understanding Your Dashboard

After login, you land on the **Dashboard** which has four tabs:

### Left Navigation

| Section | Items |
|---------|-------|
| **Dashboard** | Inbox, Active Loads, Quote Board, Call Log |
| **Autopilot** | Outbound, Agent |
| **Operations** | Load Builder, Carrier Network, Shipper Accounts, Lane Boundaries, Escalations, Analytics, Settings |

### Top Bar

- **Current page name** (left)
- **Sync button** — manually sync emails from Outlook
- **Last sync time** — shows when emails were last fetched
- **Escalation badge** — amber badge with count if open escalations exist
- **Your name + avatar** — hover over avatar to see your pod name (if assigned)
- **Sign Out**

### Bottom Stats (Left Nav)

- **Active Loads** — loads currently in progress
- **AI Actions Today** — how many AI operations ran today
- **Escalations** — open escalation count
- **Loads Delivered** — all-time delivered count

---

## 4. Inbox & Email Management

### Email Categories

The AI automatically classifies every incoming email:

| Category | What It Means |
|----------|---------------|
| **Important (IMP)** | Urgent, high priority, needs immediate attention |
| **Shipper** | Load tender or request from a shipper |
| **Tender** | Carrier rate quote or bid |
| **Status Inquiry** | Shipper asking about load status |
| **Bidding** | Email in active rate negotiation |
| **Unimportant** | Low priority, informational |
| **Ignore** | Newsletters, marketing, auto-responses |

### Category Tabs

Click any tab to filter emails. Each tab shows a count badge.

### Reading Emails

1. Click an email to open the detail page
2. The email is automatically marked as **read** in both the app and Outlook
3. You'll see:
   - Full email body (HTML rendered)
   - AI reasoning (why it was classified this way)
   - Confidence score (%)
   - Conversation thread (if part of a chain)

### Actions on an Email

- **Retag with AI** — re-run classification if you think it's wrong
- **Change category** — manually override the AI's classification
- **Custom tag** — add your own label (e.g., "Rush", "VIP Shipper")
- **Generate reply** — AI drafts a contextual response (for Shipper/Status emails)
- **Delete** — move to trash

### Composing New Emails

1. Click the **+** button next to the search bar
2. Fill in To, CC/BCC (optional), Subject, Body
3. Use the **"Improve Writing"** button for AI-assisted editing
4. **Ctrl+Enter** to send

### Hide Ignored Emails

Go to the **Ignore** tab and toggle **"Hide ignored from All tab"**. This removes ignored emails from the All tab so you only see what matters. The setting persists across sessions.

---

## 5. Working with Loads

### Viewing Loads

1. Go to **Load Builder** in the left nav (or **Active Loads** tab on dashboard)
2. Use the status filter: All, Pending, Sourcing, Booked, In Transit, Delivered
3. Click any load to see full details

### Load Details Include

- Route (origin city/state to destination city/state)
- Equipment type, weight, miles
- Pickup and delivery dates
- Shipper rate vs carrier rate
- Gross profit and margin
- Assigned carrier (if booked)
- Carrier quotes from email, phone, and outbound campaigns

### Creating a New Load

1. Click **"Create Load"** on the Loads page
2. Fill in the multi-step form:
   - Basic info (client/shipper, bill type)
   - Pickup details (address, date, contact)
   - Delivery details (address, date, contact)
   - Commodities (items, weight, class)
   - Equipment and rate
3. Submit — the load is created in TTMS

**Pod scoping**: If you're in a pod, the load is created with your `user_id` in TTMS, which means it belongs to your pod. Only users in the same pod will see it.

### Creating a Shipper

When you create a shipper via the Shippers page, the same pod scoping applies — the shipper is created with your `user_id` in TTMS and scoped to your pod.

---

## 6. Carrier Network

### Viewing Carriers

1. Go to **Carrier Network** in the left nav
2. Search by carrier name
3. Browse the paginated list

### Carrier Information

Each carrier shows: name, contact, email, phone, MC number, DOT number, equipment types, and active/inactive status.

### Adding a Carrier

1. Click **"Add Carrier"**
2. Fill in: company name, contact, email, phone, MC#, DOT#, equipment types
3. Submit

Carriers are shared across all users (not pod-scoped) since carriers work with multiple brokers.

---

## 7. Shipper Accounts

### Viewing Shippers

1. Go to **Shipper Accounts** in the left nav
2. Search by shipper name
3. Browse the paginated list

### Adding a Shipper

1. Click **"Add Shipper"**
2. Fill in: company name, billing email, phone, address, contact
3. Submit

**Pod scoping**: Shippers created by pod users are linked to their pod via `user_id` in TTMS.

---

## 8. Quote Board

The Quote Board shows all loads that have active carrier quotes, bids, or negotiations.

### What Populates the Quote Board

- **Carrier rate quote emails** — when a carrier emails you with a rate
- **Outbound campaign responses** — when carriers respond to your offers
- **VAPI call extractions** — when AI phone calls result in rate quotes

### Viewing the Quote Board

1. Go to the **Quote Board** tab on the dashboard
2. Each card shows: load ID, route, best rate, quote count, latest activity
3. Click a load to see all quotes for that load

### Quote Detail

For each load, you'll see every carrier quote with:
- Source (Inbound email, Outbound offer, Phone call)
- Carrier name and MC number
- Quoted rate
- Status (Quoted, Negotiating, Accepted, Rejected)
- Negotiation round count
- Margin to shipper rate

### Pod Scoping on Quote Board

If you're in a pod, the quote board only shows loads belonging to your pod. Loads from other pods are hidden. Users without a pod see everything.

---

## 9. Outbound — Carrier Offers

The Outbound page lets you proactively reach out to carriers with rate offers for your pending loads.

### How to Send Carrier Offers

1. Go to **Outbound** in the left nav
2. Search for a load by number, or browse pending loads
3. Expand a load card to see the **Carrier Scoring Panel**
4. The panel ranks carriers by:
   - Lane history (past bookings on this route)
   - Response rate (% of offers they replied to)
   - Acceptance behavior (% of replies that were acceptances)
   - Equipment match
   - Ghost carrier detection (inactive carriers ranked lower)
5. Click **"Send Offer"** for the carriers you want to contact
6. The AI generates a personalized email with load details and your rate
7. The email is sent via your Outlook account

### After Sending

- The offer appears on the Quote Board as an "Outbound" quote
- When the carrier replies, their response shows up in your inbox as a TENDER email
- The negotiation is tracked on the Quote Board with round counts

---

## 10. Call Log (VAPI)

The Call Log shows all AI-powered phone calls made through the VAPI system.

### What You See

- Phone number called
- Call duration
- Status (Ended, Failed, etc.)
- AI-generated summary of the conversation
- Full transcript (expandable)
- Order number tag (auto-detected from transcript)
- Listen to recording link

### How Calls Happen

VAPI calls are triggered by the agent system or manually. The AI negotiates with carriers over the phone, and results are extracted and fed into the Quote Board.

---

## 11. Lane Boundaries

Lane Boundaries are rate guardrails that prevent the AI from accepting or offering rates outside your acceptable range.

### Setting Up Boundaries

1. Go to **Lane Boundaries** in the left nav
2. Click **"Add Lane"**
3. Enter:
   - Lane description (e.g., "Chicago to Dallas")
   - Origin state and destination state
   - Equipment type
4. Set rates manually, or click **"AI Suggest"** to get market-based recommendations:
   - **Floor** — absolute minimum rate you'd accept
   - **Target** — ideal rate you're aiming for
   - **Ceiling** — absolute maximum you'd pay a carrier

### How They're Used

- When the agent generates an offer, it checks lane boundaries
- If a carrier's rate exceeds the ceiling, an **escalation** is created
- If a rate is below the floor, the agent pauses negotiation
- The AI shows its reasoning when suggesting rates

---

## 12. Escalations

Escalations are issues that need your attention — situations where the AI hit a safety rail or something unusual happened.

### Common Triggers

- Rate exceeds lane ceiling
- No carrier coverage for a load
- Late pickup risk detected
- Carrier dropped a load
- Low AI confidence on a decision
- Shipper complaint detected

### Handling Escalations

1. Go to **Escalations** in the left nav
2. You'll see open escalations with:
   - Trigger type and description
   - Load context (route, carrier, rate)
   - AI recommendation
3. Choose an action to resolve (e.g., "Approve", "Contact Shipper", "Skip Carrier")
4. The escalation is marked resolved and the agent continues

### Scan TTMS

Click **"Scan TTMS"** to manually check for new issues across all orders (late pickups, delivery problems, disputed rates).

---

## 13. Agent Mode (Autopilot)

The Agent is the AI autopilot that can process emails, send offers, and negotiate with carriers autonomously.

### Modes

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| **Off** | Agent disabled, nothing runs automatically |
| **Test** | Everything runs but all outbound emails are held as drafts for your review |
| **Live** | Full autonomous mode — emails send immediately |

### The Agent Pipeline

1. **Sync & Classify** — Pulls new emails, classifies them
2. **Extract & Quote** — Extracts load details, generates rate quotes for tenders
3. **Carrier Blast** — Scores carriers and sends personalized offers
4. **Negotiate & Book** — Handles carrier replies, counters, accepts best offer
5. **Safety Rails** — Rate ceilings, max rounds, dangerous keyword detection
6. **Learning Loop** — Logs decisions, learns from outcomes
7. **Sweep & Catch-Up** — Checks for unreplied emails that slipped through

### Running the Agent

1. Go to **Agent** in the left nav
2. Select your mode (Test recommended for first use)
3. Click **"Run Now"**
4. Watch the live activity stream as the agent works

### Reviewing Drafts (Test Mode)

In Test mode, the agent creates drafts instead of sending:
1. Drafts appear in the sidebar
2. Review each draft: recipient, subject, body
3. Click **Approve** to send, or **Discard** to skip

---

## 14. Analytics

### Available Analytics

1. **Load Pipeline** — pending, active, and delivered load counts
2. **Email Activity** — emails per day, category breakdown (pie chart)
3. **Negotiation Performance** — win rate, average rounds, best rates
4. **VAPI Call Performance** — calls per day, conversion rates
5. **AI Cost Tracking** — LLM spend per day, by model, by purpose

### Time Periods

Use the 7/30/90 day buttons to change the analysis window.

---

## 15. Settings

### What You Can Configure

- **Auto-classify emails** — toggle on/off
- **Sync interval** — how often emails sync (5/15/30/60 minutes)
- **Important keywords** — words that force emails to be classified as Important
- **Ignore domains** — domains whose emails are always classified as Ignore
- **Dark mode** — toggle appearance

---

## 16. Pod Scoping — How It Works

Pods are organizational groups in TTMS. They control who sees what.

### For Users IN a Pod

| Feature | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| **Loads** | Only see loads from your pod |
| **Shippers** | Only see shippers assigned to your pod |
| **Quote Board** | Only see quotes for loads in your pod |
| **Creating a Load** | Load is created in your pod (via `user_id`) |
| **Creating a Shipper** | Shipper is created in your pod (via `user_id`) |
| **Carriers** | See all carriers (carriers are shared) |
| **Call Log** | See all calls (calls are shared) |
| **Inbox** | See your own emails only (always per-user) |

### For Users NOT in a Pod

Everything is visible — no filtering applied. This is typically for admins or managers who need the full picture.

### How Pod Detection Works

1. On login, the system looks up your email in TTMS
2. If found, your `ttms_user_id` and `ttms_pod_title` are saved to your profile
3. Every API call that fetches loads or shippers passes your `user_id` to TTMS
4. TTMS returns only data belonging to your pod
5. The Quote Board tags each load as "in pod" or "not in pod" during sync

---

## 17. Admin Dashboard

The admin dashboard is a separate interface for monitoring all users and system health.

### Access

- URL: `/admin`
- Username: `admin`
- Password: `Admin1234+`
- This is NOT connected to Supabase auth — it's a standalone login

### Overview Tab

- Total users, active today, total emails
- LLM calls and cost today
- VAPI extractions today
- Active email connections
- **User table**: Click any user to see their full detail (emails, LLM usage, quote board, connection status)

### Analytics Tab

Charts and graphs covering:
- LLM cost per day (bar chart, 30 days)
- Emails per day (bar chart, 30 days)
- LLM calls per day (line chart)
- VAPI extractions per day
- Email category breakdown (pie chart)
- Load status breakdown (pie chart)
- Negotiation status (pie chart)
- LLM cost by purpose (horizontal bar — which AI features cost most)
- LLM cost by model (horizontal bar — which models cost most)
- Financial summary: total revenue, carrier costs, gross profit, margin %

---

## 18. Common Workflows

### Workflow 1: Processing a Shipper Tender

1. New email arrives in Inbox, classified as **Shipper**
2. Open the email
3. Click **"Generate Reply"** — AI drafts a response with rate quote
4. Review and edit the draft
5. Click **Send**
6. The shipper reply is sent via your Outlook

### Workflow 2: Sending Carrier Offers

1. Go to **Outbound**
2. Find a pending load
3. Expand it to see scored carriers
4. Click **"Send Offer"** for top carriers
5. AI emails are sent with your rate and load details
6. Track responses on the **Quote Board**

### Workflow 3: Handling a Carrier Rate Quote

1. Carrier emails you with a rate
2. Email appears in Inbox as **Tender**
3. Rate is automatically tracked on the **Quote Board**
4. If rate is acceptable, book the carrier
5. If not, the agent can counter-offer (in autopilot mode)

### Workflow 4: Setting Up a New Lane

1. Go to **Lane Boundaries**
2. Click **"Add Lane"**
3. Enter origin, destination, equipment
4. Click **"AI Suggest"** for market-rate recommendations
5. Adjust floor/target/ceiling as needed
6. Save — the agent will respect these bounds

### Workflow 5: Reviewing Agent Activity

1. Go to **Agent**
2. Set mode to **Test** (drafts only, nothing sends)
3. Click **"Run Now"**
4. Watch the live stream of AI actions
5. Review drafts in the sidebar
6. Approve good ones, discard bad ones
7. Once confident, switch to **Live** mode

---

## Quick Reference

| Action | Where |
|--------|-------|
| Sync emails | Top bar Sync button |
| Compose email | + button in Inbox |
| Create load | Load Builder > Create Load |
| Add carrier | Carrier Network > Add Carrier |
| Add shipper | Shipper Accounts > Add Shipper |
| Send carrier offer | Outbound > expand load > Send Offer |
| Set rate boundaries | Lane Boundaries > Add Lane |
| View escalations | Escalations (badge shows count) |
| Run agent | Agent > select mode > Run Now |
| View analytics | Analytics page |
| Change settings | Settings page |
| Admin dashboard | /admin |

FILE: who_am_I.md
================================================
# Red Dog Mailer — AI Freight Broker That Never Sleeps

> **One-liner:** An autonomous AI agent that reads emails, extracts loads, scores carriers, negotiates rates, and books freight — while you focus on relationships.

---

## The Problem

A freight broker's day looks like this:

1. Open Outlook. 200+ emails.
2. Scan for tenders (bid requests), shipper load awards, and carrier replies.
3. Manually extract load details from emails and PDFs — origin, destination, rate, weight, pickup date.
4. Search for carriers who've run that lane before. Call or email them.
5. Negotiate rates back and forth over email. Track who countered what.
6. First carrier to accept wins. Cancel the rest.
7. Create the order in the TMS. Send confirmations.
8. Repeat 50-100 times per day.

Every step is manual. Every step is a place where loads fall through the cracks, carriers ghost you, and money gets left on the table.

**Red Dog Mailer replaces steps 1-7 with an AI agent that runs continuously, learns from every negotiation, and never forgets to follow up.**

---

## What It Does

### The 60-Second Version

Emails arrive in Outlook. The agent syncs them, classifies them with AI, extracts load details from the body and PDF attachments, scores carriers based on lane history and past behavior, generates personalized offer emails, negotiates rates autonomously within your guardrails, and books the load in TTMS when a carrier accepts. Every decision is logged. Every outcome feeds a learning loop that makes the next negotiation sharper.

You review drafts, approve sends, and handle the 5% that needs a human touch. The agent handles the other 95%.

### The Full Pipeline

```
                          ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
                          │         OUTLOOK INBOX            │
                          │    (Microsoft Graph Delta API)   │
                          └──────────────┬──────────────────┘
                                         │
                                    ┌────▼────┐
                                    │  SYNC   │  Pull new emails, classify with AI
                                    └────┬────┘
                                         │
                    ┌────────────────────┼────────────────────┐
                    │                    │                     │
              ┌─────▼─────┐       ┌─────▼─────┐       ┌──────▼──────┐
              │  TENDER    │       │  SHIPPER   │       │  IMPORTANT  │
              │ (bid req)  │       │ (new load) │       │ (carrier)   │
              └─────┬──────┘       └─────┬──────┘       └──────┬──────┘
                    │                    │                      │
           Extract lanes         Extract load data       Match to active
           from email+PDF        from email+PDF          carrier blast
                    │                    │                      │
           Generate AI           Create order             ┌────▼────┐
           rate quotes           in TTMS                  │ INTENT  │
                    │                    │                 │ DETECT  │
           Send CSV reply        Send shipper             └────┬────┘
           to shipper            confirmation                  │
                    │                    │              ┌───────┼───────┐
           Track as              ┌──────▼──────┐       │       │       │
           "bidding"             │   CARRIER    │    Accept  Counter  Walk
                    │            │    BLAST     │       │       │     Away
           Await                 └──────┬──────┘       │       │       │
           award email                  │              Book  Negotiate  Escalate
                    │            Score carriers          │    within     or
           If awarded:           by lane history         │   ceiling    re-blast
           convert to            + response rate         │       │
           shipper flow                 │                │       │
                                 Generate AI             └───┬───┘
                                 offer emails                │
                                        │              ┌─────▼─────┐
                                 Send to top N         │  LEARNING  │
                                 carriers              │   LOOP     │
                                        │              └───────────┘
                                 Track as               Every outcome
                                 carrier_blasts         feeds future
                                        │               AI prompts
                                 4-hour expiry
                                 then re-blast
                                 to fresh carriers
```

---

## Feature Deep Dive

### 1. Email Intelligence

**Sync:** Microsoft Graph delta API pulls new emails in real-time via webhooks + periodic delta sync. No polling lag. No missed emails.

**Classification:** AI classifies every email into five categories:
- **TENDER** — Rate quote requests / RFPs from shippers
- **SHIPPER** — Load awards, booking confirmations, shipment instructions
- **IMP** (Important) — Carrier replies, rate counters, acceptance emails
- **UNIMP** — FYI emails, status updates, non-actionable
- **IGNORE** — Marketing, newsletters, spam

Classification runs on Gemini Flash Lite ($0.25/1M tokens) — fast and cheap enough to classify every email in the inbox.

### 2. Load Extraction (Email + PDF)

The AI reads email bodies and PDF attachments to extract structured load data:

| Field | Example |
|-------|---------|
| Origin | Kankakee, IL |
| Destination | Willoughby, OH |
| Shipper Rate | $1,600 |
| Equipment | TL (Truckload) |
| Weight | 46,000 lbs |
| Pickup Date | 2026-03-26 |
| Commodity | SHINGLES |
| Requirements | STRAPS, EDGE PROTECTORS & TARPS |

Handles multi-load emails (one email, 5 loads). Handles tender PDFs with lane tables. Confidence scored — low confidence triggers human escalation instead of silent failure.

**Model:** Gemini 3 Flash ($0.50/$3.00 per 1M tokens) — proven for structured extraction with PDF multimodal support.

### 3. Carrier Scoring Engine

Every carrier is scored on a **100-point scale** using data from TTMS:

| Factor | Points | Logic |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Lane History | 35 | 6+ loads = 35, 4+ = 28, 2+ = 20, 1 = 10 |
| Rating | 25 | SATISFACTORY or CONDITIONAL = 25 |
| Approval | 15 | Approved = 15 |
| Insurance | 15 | Valid = 15 |
| Active Status | 10 | Active = 10 |

**Plus response-aware adjustments from blast history:**
- Response rate > 80%: **+15 pts** (reliable carrier)
- Response rate < 20%: **-10 pts** (low engagement)
- Acceptance rate > 50%: **+5 pts** (deal closer)
- 3+ consecutive ghosted blasts: **-15 pts** (deprioritize)

Top N carriers (configurable, default 3) get personalized AI offer emails that reference their specific lane history, last rate, and relationship context. Opens at target rate minus 10% to leave room for negotiation.

### 4. AI Negotiation Engine

When a carrier replies, the AI:

1. **Detects intent** — accept, counter, walk away, question, provide MC number
2. **Validates rate** — counter must be within ceiling (configurable, default 15% above target)
3. **Auto-accepts** — if carrier's counter is at or below target rate
4. **Counter-offers** — generates a human-sounding reply with rate context and learning injections
5. **Escalates** — if max rounds reached, rate above ceiling, or dangerous keywords detected

**Negotiation replies send instantly** — no draft gate. In freight, speed wins loads. A 2-minute delay means the carrier moved on.

**Learning injection:** Before generating each counter-offer, the AI receives:
- This carrier's historical acceptance patterns
- Average discount they negotiate on this lane
- Shipper's typical award behavior
- Lane-level rate trends

### 5. Proactive Reblast (Unbooked Load Coverage)

The agent doesn't just react to emails — it actively monitors for unbooked loads:

**Source 1 — TTMS PENDING Orders:**
- Fetches recent PENDING orders from TTMS where `carrier_id = null`
- These are loads sitting in the TMS with no carrier assigned
- Scores past carriers on the lane, generates offer emails, blasts top candidates

**Source 2 — Failed Blasts:**
- Finds loads where ALL carrier blasts expired or were declined
- Excludes already-contacted carriers
- Re-blasts to the next best-scored carriers

Capped at 3 new + 3 re-blasts per cycle to avoid runaway.

### 6. Tender Quote Pipeline

When a shipper sends a rate request:

1. Extract lanes from email + PDF attachments
2. Check for duplicates (same shipper, 70%+ lane overlap in 24h → escalate)
3. Generate AI rate quotes using lane boundaries (floor/target/ceiling)
4. Build a CSV rate sheet
5. Reply to shipper with CSV attachment
6. Track as `quoted_loads` with status `bidding`
7. Monitor for award email → convert to order → trigger carrier blast

### 7. Race Manager (First-Come-First-Served)

Multiple carriers get blasted for the same load. First to accept wins:

- Winner gets booking confirmation + TTMS auto-updated
- All other carriers get a courtesy "load covered" email
- Late acceptors are rejected gracefully (preserves relationship for next load)
- Blast records updated: winner = `accepted`, others = `cancelled`

### 8. Safety Rails

| Guard | What It Does |
|-------|-------------|
| Rate Ceiling | Blocks negotiation above target + ceiling% |
| Max Rounds | Stops after N counter-offers (default 3) |
| Low Confidence | Escalates if AI extraction < 80% confident |
| Keyword Detection | Escalates on "lawsuit", "attorney", "fraud", "subpoena" |
| Circuit Breaker | 3+ escalations in 1 hour → auto-pauses agent |
| Ghost Penalty | -15 pts for carriers who ignore 3+ consecutive blasts |

### 9. Learning Loop

Every decision and outcome is captured:

- **Negotiation events:** intent, rates offered/countered, carrier context
- **Quote tracking:** quote → award conversion rate by shipper and lane
- **Carrier behavior:** acceptance rates, average discount negotiated, ghost frequency
- **Pattern distillation:** nightly cron distills raw events into carrier/lane/shipper learnings

These learnings are injected into AI prompts before every negotiation reply and offer email, creating a feedback loop that gets sharper over time.

### 10. Draft Management & Test Mode

**Three operating modes:**

| Mode | Emails | Orders | Blasts | Drafts |
|------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| **Off** | Sync only | None | None | None |
| **Test** | Processed | Simulated | Drafted (is_test=true) | Reviewable, can't send |
| **Live** | Processed | Created in TTMS | Sent or drafted | Sendable |

**Draft Mode (within Live):** All outbound emails except negotiation replies are queued for human review. Negotiation replies skip the gate — speed matters.

**Test Mode:** The full pipeline runs — TTMS data fetched, carriers scored, AI generates emails — but nothing goes out. Drafts are created with `is_test=true` and auto-deleted when test mode is disabled. LLM costs are tracked even in test mode.

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Why |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| **Framework** | Next.js 16.1.6 (Turbopack) | App Router, API routes, SSE streaming, server components |
| **Database** | Supabase (PostgreSQL) | RLS, realtime subscriptions, service role for agent |
| **AI** | OpenRouter → Gemini 3 Flash | $0.50/$3.00 per 1M tokens. Fast (3s), cheap, proven for extraction + negotiation |
| **Email** | Microsoft Graph API | Delta sync, webhooks, send/reply, PDF attachment fetch |
| **TMS** | Red Dog TTMS API | Orders, carriers, clients, past carrier data |
| **Voice** | VAPI | Call transcript extraction for rate/order data |
| **Deployment** | Vercel | Serverless functions (300s max), cron jobs, edge caching |
| **Auth** | Supabase Auth + Azure AD | Microsoft OAuth for Outlook access |
| **UI** | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui + Recharts | Dashboard, analytics charts, realtime feeds |

### AI Model Strategy

| Task | Model | Speed | Cost |
|------|-------|-------|------|
| Email classification | Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite | ~1s | $0.25/$1.50 per 1M |
| Intent parsing | Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite | ~1s | $0.25/$1.50 per 1M |
| Load extraction (email+PDF) | Gemini 3 Flash | ~3s | $0.50/$3.00 per 1M |
| Negotiation replies | Gemini 3 Flash | ~3s | $0.50/$3.00 per 1M |
| Offer emails | Gemini 3 Flash | ~3s | $0.50/$3.00 per 1M |
| Pattern distillation | Gemini 3 Flash | ~5s | $0.50/$3.00 per 1M |

**Estimated cost per agent cycle:** ~$0.01 (20 emails classified, 1 extraction, 3 offer emails)
**Monthly at 24/7 every 2 min:** ~$7/month

---

## Database Schema (22 migrations)

### Core Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `profiles` | User accounts (extends Supabase auth) |
| `email_connections` | Microsoft OAuth tokens per tenant |
| `emails` | Synced inbox (classified, tagged, processed) |
| `email_replies` | Sent replies (manual + agent) |
| `email_negotiations` | Rate negotiation state per carrier thread |
| `loads` | Cached load data (from TTMS + extracted) |
| `carriers` | Carrier directory (synced from TTMS) |
| `shippers` | Shipper/client directory |
| `carrier_quotes` | Rate quotes from carriers |
| `rate_parameters` | Lane-level floor/target/ceiling rates |
| `escalations` | Safety escalation queue |
| `sync_state` | Delta sync cursors per tenant |

### Agent Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `agent_settings` | Per-user config (enabled, test_mode, draft_mode, max_blast, etc.) |
| `agent_activity` | Detailed cycle event log (phase, timing, cycle_id) |
| `agent_drafts` | Pending outbound emails (test + live, reviewable) |
| `carrier_blasts` | Blast tracking (carrier, score, offer_rate, status, expiry) |

### Analytics Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `ai_activity_log` | Dashboard activity counter + LLM cost tracking (cost, model, tokens, latency) |
| `llm_usage` | Legacy LLM usage (kept for backward compat) |
| `negotiation_events` | Full negotiation history with context snapshots |
| `quoted_loads` | Quote lifecycle (bidding → awarded → expired) |
| `vapi_call_extractions` | Structured data from phone call transcripts |

| `/api/cron/extract-vapi` | Daily 3pm | Extract data from call transcripts |

---

## Dashboard Pages

### Inbox
Full email client with classification badges, category filters, and one-click AI reply generation. Click an email to see the full thread, extract loads, or generate a negotiation counter-offer.

### Agent
Control center with mode selector (Off/Test/Live), live console with SSE streaming, pending drafts sidebar with edit/send/discard, and real-time activity feed via Supabase subscriptions.

### Outbound
Compose and send new emails to carriers with AI-generated offer emails. Carrier scoring panel shows scores, lane history, and suggested rates.

### Loads
Load directory synced from TTMS. View load details, assigned carriers, negotiation history, and status tracking.

### Carriers
Carrier directory with scoring, blast history, response rates, and contact info. Linked to TTMS carrier profiles.

### Shippers
Shipper/client directory with award rates, preferred lanes, and rate history.

### Quote Board
Visual board of all quoted loads — bidding, awarded, expired. VAPI call data integration for phone-sourced quotes.

### Lane Boundaries
Configure floor/target/ceiling rates per lane. AI-assisted boundary generation based on historical data.

### Escalations
Queue of issues requiring human attention — low confidence extractions, rate ceiling breaches, ghosted loads, keyword alerts.

### Analytics
Comprehensive dashboard with:
- **Email stats** — volume by category, top senders, daily trends
- **Negotiation metrics** — win rate, avg rounds, best rate, rate distribution
- **VAPI call analysis** — intent breakdown, booking conversion rate
- **AI cost tracking** — spend by purpose, model, daily cost chart
- **Load metrics** — active, pending, delivered counts from TTMS

---

## What Makes This Different

### 1. It's Not a Chatbot
This isn't "ask AI to draft an email." It's a closed-loop autonomous system that processes an entire inbox, makes decisions, takes actions, and learns from outcomes — without human intervention for 95% of cases.

### 2. The Learning Loop Is Real
Most AI tools are stateless — they don't remember yesterday's negotiation. Red Dog Mailer captures every decision and outcome, distills them into carrier/lane/shipper patterns, and injects them into future prompts. The agent gets measurably better over time.

### 3. Speed = Money
In freight, the first broker to respond gets the load. The agent processes emails in seconds, not minutes. Negotiation replies are instant. Carrier blasts go out before a human would finish reading the email.

### 4. Safety By Default
Rate ceilings, max rounds, confidence thresholds, keyword detection, circuit breaker — the agent has guardrails at every step. Test mode lets you watch the full pipeline run without anything going out. Draft mode lets you review before sends.

### 5. It Uses Your TMS
Not a replacement for TTMS — an extension. Orders are created in TTMS. Carrier data comes from TTMS. Past carrier history on lanes comes from TTMS. The agent enhances the workflow you already have.

---

## Numbers

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Source files | 196 TypeScript files |
| API endpoints | 45+ routes |
| Database tables | 22+ (across 22 migrations) |
| Cron jobs | 8 scheduled tasks |
| AI model calls per cycle | 10-20 |
| Cost per cycle | ~$0.01 |
| Monthly AI cost (24/7) | ~$7 |
| Agent cycle time | 30-90 seconds |
| Carrier scoring factors | 5 static + 4 dynamic |
| Negotiation learning tables | 3 (events, quotes, patterns) |

---

## The Bottom Line

A single freight broker handles 50-100 loads per day. Each load requires 5-15 emails, 2-4 carrier contacts, and 1-3 negotiation rounds. That's 500-1,500 email interactions per day, per broker.

Red Dog Mailer handles the repetitive 95% — classify, extract, score, blast, negotiate, book — so the broker can focus on the 5% that actually needs a human: relationship building, complex disputes, and strategic decisions.

**It's not replacing the broker. It's giving them superpowers.**



================================================
FILE: .env.example
================================================
# ============================================================
# Red Dog Mailer — Environment Variables
# Copy this file to .env.local and fill in your values
# ============================================================

# ── Supabase ──
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=your-service-role-key

# ── Microsoft Azure AD (Outlook OAuth) ──
AZURE_AD_CLIENT_ID=your-azure-app-client-id
AZURE_AD_CLIENT_SECRET=your-azure-app-client-secret
AZURE_AD_TENANT_ID=common

# ── Microsoft Graph Webhooks ──
GRAPH_WEBHOOK_CLIENT_STATE=your-random-secret-string

# ── OpenRouter (AI / LLM) ──
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your-openrouter-api-key

# ── Red Dog TTMS API ──
REDDOG_TTMS_BASE_URL=https://reddog.t-tms.com
REDDOG_TTMS_API_KEY=your-ttms-api-key

# ── VAPI (Voice AI) ──
VAPI_API_KEY=your-vapi-api-key

# ── Vercel Cron Secret ──
CRON_SECRET=your-cron-secret

# ── App URL ──
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000
