// ============================================================
// TTMS Mailer — Shared Activity Logger
// Logs all user-visible actions to ai_activity_log table.
// Non-fatal: errors are caught and logged, never thrown.
// ============================================================

import { createServiceClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";

interface LogActivityParams {
  tenantId: string;
  actionType: string;
  description: string;
  emailId?: string | null;
  loadId?: string | null;
  carrierId?: string | null;
  shipperId?: string | null;
  outcome?: string | null;
  metadata?: Record<string, unknown> | null;
}

/**
 * Log an activity to the ai_activity_log table.
 * This is fire-and-forget — errors are caught internally.
 */
export async function logActivity(params: LogActivityParams): Promise<void> {
  try {
    const supabase = await createServiceClient();
    await supabase.from("ai_activity_log").insert({
      tenant_id: params.tenantId,
      action_type: params.actionType,
      action_description: params.description,
      email_id: params.emailId || null,
      load_id: params.loadId || null,
      carrier_id: params.carrierId || null,
      shipper_id: params.shipperId || null,
      outcome: params.outcome || null,
      metadata: params.metadata || null,
    });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("[activity-log] Failed to log activity:", err);
  }
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/admin-auth.ts
================================================
/**
 * Simple admin authentication — hardcoded credentials.
 * Not Supabase auth. Admin token stored in sessionStorage on the client.
 */

const ADMIN_USER = "admin";
const ADMIN_PASS = "Admin1234+";

// Simple token: base64 of credentials + timestamp. Not a JWT — just enough to verify.
export function generateAdminToken(): string {
  const payload = `${ADMIN_USER}:${Date.now()}`;
  return Buffer.from(payload).toString("base64");
}

export function validateCredentials(username: string, password: string): boolean {
  return username === ADMIN_USER && password === ADMIN_PASS;
}

export function validateAdminToken(token: string | null): boolean {
  if (!token) return false;
  try {
    const decoded = Buffer.from(token, "base64").toString();
    return decoded.startsWith(`${ADMIN_USER}:`);
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

/**
 * Check admin auth from request headers.
 * Expects: Authorization: Bearer <token>
 */
export function isAdminAuthed(request: Request): boolean {
  const auth = request.headers.get("authorization");
  if (!auth?.startsWith("Bearer ")) return false;
  return validateAdminToken(auth.slice(7));
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/award-detection.ts
================================================
// ============================================================
// TTMS Mailer — Award Detection for Follow-Up Emails
// Checks if a new email is an award for a previously quoted load.
// Called by webhook and email-sync after classification.
// ============================================================

import { createServiceClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";
import { logActivity } from "@/lib/activity-log";
import { openrouterCall } from "@/lib/ai/openrouter-client";
import { logQuoteEvent } from "@/lib/learning";

const MODEL = "google/gemini-3-flash-preview";

const AWARD_DETECTION_PROMPT = `You are a freight logistics analyst. You are looking at a FOLLOW-UP email in a conversation where a broker previously sent rate quotes to a shipper.

Determine if this follow-up email is the shipper AWARDING the load(s) to the broker.

**AWARDED (is_awarded = true):**
- The shipper is accepting the quoted rates or assigning loads
- Contains: "We'd like to proceed", "Load awarded", "Please accept", "Confirmed", "You got it", "Awarded to you", "We accept your rates", "Load assigned"
- References specific load numbers, PO numbers, or lanes from the previous quote
- The tone is confirmatory/directive — telling the broker the load is theirs
- Load tender documents or rate confirmations attached
- Automated TMS notifications assigning the load

**NOT AWARDED (is_awarded = false):**
- The shipper is asking for revised rates or more information
- The shipper is declining: "We went with another carrier", "Not at this time"
- General follow-up questions about availability or terms
- Auto-replies, read receipts, or acknowledgment emails ("Thanks for your quote")
- Asking to re-quote at different rates

Respond with ONLY a JSON object (no markdown):
{
  "is_awarded": true/false,
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
  "reasoning": "one sentence explanation"
}`;

interface AwardCheckResult {
  is_awarded: boolean;
  confidence: number;
  reasoning: string;
}

/**
 * Run AI award detection on a single email.
 */
async function checkAward(email: {
  subject: string | null;
  from_name: string | null;
  from_address: string;
  body_preview: string | null;
}): Promise<AwardCheckResult | null> {
  try {
    const emailText = `From: ${email.from_name || email.from_address}
Subject: ${email.subject || "(no subject)"}

${email.body_preview || ""}`;

    const result = await openrouterCall({
      model: MODEL,
      temperature: 0.1,
      timeoutMs: 15000,
      purpose: "detect_award",
      messages: [
        { role: "system", content: AWARD_DETECTION_PROMPT },
        { role: "user", content: emailText },
      ],
    });

    const jsonMatch = result.content.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/);
    if (!jsonMatch) return null;

    const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
    return {
      is_awarded: parsed.is_awarded === true,
      confidence: typeof parsed.confidence === "number" ? parsed.confidence : 0,
      reasoning: parsed.reasoning || "",
    };
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("[award-detection] AI check failed:", err);
    return null;
  }
}

/**
 * Check if a newly arrived email is an award for a previously quoted load.
 * Called after email classification in webhook and email-sync.
 *
 * Returns true if an award was detected and persisted.
 */
export async function detectAwardOnFollowUp(
  userId: string,
  emailId: string,
  conversationId: string | null,
  email: {
    subject: string | null;
    from_name: string | null;
    from_address: string;
    body_preview: string | null;
  }
): Promise<boolean> {
  if (!conversationId) return false;

  const supabase = await createServiceClient();

  // Check if there's a quoted_load in 'bidding' status for this conversation
  const { data: quotedLoads } = await supabase
    .from("quoted_loads")
    .select("id, email_id, shipper_name, lanes, email_subject")
    .eq("user_id", userId)
    .eq("conversation_id", conversationId)
    .eq("awarded_status", "bidding");

  if (!quotedLoads || quotedLoads.length === 0) return false;

  console.log(
    `[award-detection] Found ${quotedLoads.length} bidding load(s) for conversation ${conversationId}, checking email ${emailId}`
  );

  // Run AI award check
  const result = await checkAward(email);
  if (!result) return false;

  console.log(
    `[award-detection] Result: is_awarded=${result.is_awarded}, confidence=${result.confidence}, reasoning=${result.reasoning}`
  );

  if (!result.is_awarded || result.confidence < 0.7) return false;

  // Award detected! Update all quoted_loads for this conversation
  const now = new Date().toISOString();

  for (const ql of quotedLoads) {
    await supabase
      .from("quoted_loads")
      .update({
        awarded_status: "awarded",
        awarded_at: now,
        awarded_email_id: emailId,
        award_confidence: result.confidence,
        award_reasoning: result.reasoning,
      })
      .eq("id", ql.id);
  }

  // Update the original quoted email's awarded_status
  const originalEmailIds = quotedLoads.map((ql) => ql.email_id);
  for (const origId of originalEmailIds) {
    await supabase
      .from("emails")
      .update({ awarded_status: "awarded" })
      .eq("id", origId);
  }

  // Update the new award email's awarded_status too
  await supabase
    .from("emails")
    .update({ awarded_status: "awarded" })
    .eq("id", emailId);

  // Re-classify the award email as SHIPPER (it's now an individual load assignment)
  await supabase
    .from("emails")
    .update({
      category: "SHIPPER",
      ai_reasoning: `Award detected: ${result.reasoning}`,
      confidence: result.confidence,
    })
    .eq("id", emailId);

  // Log activity
  const shipperName = quotedLoads[0]?.shipper_name || email.from_address;
  const subject = quotedLoads[0]?.email_subject || email.subject || "unknown";

  logActivity({
    tenantId: userId,
    actionType: "load_awarded",
    description: `Load awarded by ${shipperName}: "${subject}"`,
    emailId,
    outcome: "awarded",
    metadata: {
      confidence: result.confidence,
      reasoning: result.reasoning,
      quoted_load_ids: quotedLoads.map((ql) => ql.id),
      original_email_ids: originalEmailIds,
    },
  });

  console.log(
    `[award-detection] Award confirmed for ${quotedLoads.length} load(s) from ${shipperName}`
  );

  // Log quote_awarded event for learning loop — tracks conversion rates
  for (const ql of quotedLoads) {
    const lanes = (ql.lanes as { origin_state?: string; dest_state?: string; our_rate?: number; shipper_rate?: number; equipment_type?: string }[] || []);
    await logQuoteEvent({
      tenantId: userId,
      eventType: "quote_awarded",
      shipperEmail: email.from_address,
      shipperName,
      emailId: emailId,
      conversationId: conversationId,
      lanes: lanes.map((l) => ({
        origin_state: l.origin_state,
        dest_state: l.dest_state,
        our_rate: l.our_rate,
        shipper_rate: l.shipper_rate,
        equipment_type: l.equipment_type,
      })),
    });
  }

  return true;
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/carrier-scoring.ts
================================================
/**
 * Carrier scoring and offer email generation for proactive outreach.
 *
 * Scoring: 100-point scale based on lane history, rating, approval,
 * insurance, and active status from TTMS past_carriers data.
 *
 * Offer emails: AI-generated per carrier (not a template), referencing
 * their specific lane history and opening at target minus 10%.
 */

import type { TTMSPastCarrier } from "@/lib/ttms/client";
import { getDATRate } from "@/lib/ttms/client";
import { calculateRates } from "@/lib/negotiate";
import { openrouterCall } from "@/lib/ai/openrouter-client";

// ============================================================
// Scoring
// ============================================================

export interface ScoredCarrier extends TTMSPastCarrier {
  score: number;
}

/**
 * Score a carrier out of 100 based on past_carriers data.
 *
 * Weights:
 *   Lane History (loads_count)   — 35 pts
 *   Carrier Rating               — 25 pts
 *   Approval                     — 15 pts
 *   Insurance                    — 15 pts
 *   Active Status                — 10 pts
 */
export function scoreCarrier(carrier: TTMSPastCarrier): number {
  let score = 0;

  // Lane History (35 pts)
  const lc = carrier.loads_count ?? 0;
  if (lc >= 6) score += 35;
  else if (lc >= 4) score += 28;
  else if (lc >= 2) score += 20;
  else if (lc >= 1) score += 10;

  // Carrier Rating (25 pts)
  const rating = (carrier.carrier_rating ?? "").toUpperCase();
  if (rating === "SATISFACTORY" || rating === "CONDITIONAL") {
    score += 25;
  }

  // Approval (15 pts)
  if (carrier.carrier_approved === 1) score += 15;

  // Insurance (15 pts)
  if (carrier.insurance_valid === 1) score += 15;

  // Active Status (10 pts)
  if (carrier.carrier_status === 1) score += 10;

  return score;
}

/** Tailwind classes for score badge */
export function getScoreColor(score: number): string {
  if (score >= 80) return "bg-green-100 text-green-700 border-green-300";
  if (score >= 60) return "bg-amber-100 text-amber-700 border-amber-300";
  if (score >= 40) return "bg-gray-100 text-gray-700 border-gray-300";
  return "bg-red-100 text-red-700 border-red-300";
}

export function getScoreLabel(score: number): string {
  if (score >= 80) return "Excellent";
  if (score >= 60) return "Good";
  if (score >= 40) return "Fair";
  return "Poor";
}

// ============================================================
// Offer Email Generation (AI)
// ============================================================

const MODEL = "google/gemini-3-flash-preview";

/** Map TTMS equipment_id → equipment code for DAT API */
const EQUIPMENT_ID_TO_CODE: Record<number, string> = {
  1: "FLATBED",
  2: "DRYVAN",
  3: "FROZENTRUCKLOAD",
  4: "REFRIGERATEDTRUCKLOAD",
  5: "SPECIALIZED",
  6: "INTERMODAL53",
  7: "INTERMODAL40",
  8: "INTERMODAL20",
  9: "INTERMODAL20DRAYAGE",
  10: "DRYVANPARTIAL",
  11: "FLATBEDPARTIAL",
  12: "REFRIGERATEDPARTIAL",
  13: "FROZENPARTIAL",
  14: "FROZENLTL",
  15: "REFRIGERATEDLTL",
  16: "28FTPUP",
  17: "28FTREEFER",
  18: "40FTCONTAINER",
  19: "48FTFLATBED",
  20: "48FTREEFER",
  21: "48FTSIDELOAD",
  22: "48FTTRAILER",
  23: "53FTCONTAINER",
  24: "53FTFLATBED",
  25: "53FTREEFER",
  26: "53FTSIDELOAD",
  27: "53FTTRAILER",
  28: "DOUBLEDROP",
  29: "DOUBLEPUP",
  30: "LTL",
  31: "STEPDECK",
  32: "CONSTOGA",
  33: "HOTSHOT",
};

// ============================================================
// Blast Intelligence (learning loop)
// ============================================================

export interface BlastHistoryEntry {
  offer_rate: number;
  status: string;
  created_at: string;
  load_id: string;
}

export interface LaneOutcomeEntry {
  outcome: string;
  final_rate: number | null;
  shipper_rate: number | null;
  margin_pct: number | null;
}

export interface BlastIntelligence {
  carrierHistory: {
    blasts: BlastHistoryEntry[];
    totalBlasts: number;
    acceptedCount: number;
    expiredCount: number;
    counteredCount: number;
    declinedCount: number;
    avgOfferedRate: number | null;
    avgAcceptedRate: number | null;
  };
  laneIntelligence: {
    totalNegotiations: number;
    acceptedCount: number;
    avgAcceptedRate: number | null;
    avgMarginPct: number | null;
    recentOutcomes: LaneOutcomeEntry[];
  };
}

/**
 * Fetch blast history for a carrier + lane intelligence.
 * Accepts a supabase client to avoid importing server-only code.
 */
export async function fetchBlastIntelligence(
  supabase: { from: (table: string) => unknown },
  userId: string,
  carrierEmail: string,
  originState: string,
  destState: string
): Promise<BlastIntelligence> {
  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
  const sb = supabase as any;

  // Level 1: This carrier's blast history (last 10)
  const { data: carrierBlasts } = await sb
    .from("carrier_blasts")
    .select("offer_rate, status, created_at, load_id")
    .eq("user_id", userId)
    .eq("carrier_email", carrierEmail)
    .not("load_id", "like", "TEST-%")
    .order("created_at", { ascending: false })
    .limit(10);

  const blasts: BlastHistoryEntry[] = (carrierBlasts || []).map((b: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
    offer_rate: Number(b.offer_rate) || 0,
    status: String(b.status || ""),
    created_at: String(b.created_at || ""),
    load_id: String(b.load_id || ""),
  }));

  const acceptedBlasts = blasts.filter((b) => b.status === "accepted");
  const expiredBlasts = blasts.filter((b) => b.status === "expired");
  const counteredBlasts = blasts.filter((b) => b.status === "countered");
  const declinedBlasts = blasts.filter((b) => b.status === "declined");
  const allRates = blasts.map((b) => b.offer_rate).filter((r) => r > 0);
  const acceptedRates = acceptedBlasts.map((b) => b.offer_rate).filter((r) => r > 0);

  // Level 2: Lane intelligence from negotiation_events (last 20)
  const { data: laneEvents } = await sb
    .from("negotiation_events")
    .select("outcome, final_rate, shipper_rate, margin_pct")
    .eq("tenant_id", userId)
    .eq("origin_state", originState)
    .eq("dest_state", destState)
    .not("outcome", "is", null)
    .order("created_at", { ascending: false })
    .limit(20);

  const outcomes: LaneOutcomeEntry[] = (laneEvents || []).map((e: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
    outcome: String(e.outcome || ""),
    final_rate: e.final_rate != null ? Number(e.final_rate) : null,
    shipper_rate: e.shipper_rate != null ? Number(e.shipper_rate) : null,
    margin_pct: e.margin_pct != null ? Number(e.margin_pct) : null,
  }));

  const laneAccepted = outcomes.filter((o) => o.outcome === "accepted");
  const laneAcceptedRates = laneAccepted.map((o) => o.final_rate).filter((r): r is number => r != null && r > 0);
  const laneMargins = outcomes.map((o) => o.margin_pct).filter((m): m is number => m != null);

  return {
    carrierHistory: {
      blasts,
      totalBlasts: blasts.length,
      acceptedCount: acceptedBlasts.length,
      expiredCount: expiredBlasts.length,
      counteredCount: counteredBlasts.length,
      declinedCount: declinedBlasts.length,
      avgOfferedRate: allRates.length > 0 ? Math.round(allRates.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / allRates.length) : null,
      avgAcceptedRate: acceptedRates.length > 0 ? Math.round(acceptedRates.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / acceptedRates.length) : null,
    },
    laneIntelligence: {
      totalNegotiations: outcomes.length,
      acceptedCount: laneAccepted.length,
      avgAcceptedRate: laneAcceptedRates.length > 0 ? Math.round(laneAcceptedRates.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / laneAcceptedRates.length) : null,
      avgMarginPct: laneMargins.length > 0 ? Math.round(laneMargins.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / laneMargins.length * 100) / 100 : null,
      recentOutcomes: outcomes,
    },
  };
}

// ============================================================
// Offer Email Params
// ============================================================

export interface OfferEmailParams {
  carrierName: string;
  carrierEmail: string;
  carrierLoadsCount: number;
  carrierLastRate: number;
  /** DAT market rate at the time the carrier last moved this lane */
  carrierLastDATRate: number | null;
  carrierLastShipmentDate: string;
  loadNumber: string;
  originCity: string;
  originState: string;
  destCity: string;
  destState: string;
  equipmentType: string;
  equipmentId: number | null;
  pickupDate: string | null;
  deliveryDate: string | null;
  weightLbs: number | null;
  commodity: string | null;
  miles: number | null;
  shipperRate: number;
  senderName: string;
  /** Blast intelligence for learning loop — optional, falls back to formula if missing */
  blastIntelligence?: BlastIntelligence;
}

/**
 * Callback for reporting generation progress steps to the caller.
 * Used by the UI to show "Getting DAT rates... ✓" etc.
 */
export type OfferEmailProgressCallback = (step: {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  status: "running" | "done" | "error";
}) => void;

/**
 * AI-generated custom offer email for a specific carrier.
 * The LLM decides the rate dynamically based on all available data:
 * DAT market price, old DAT, carrier's last rate, blast history, lane patterns.
 * Server-side guardrails enforce a hard 10% minimum margin.
 */
export async function generateOfferEmail(
  params: OfferEmailParams,
  onProgress?: OfferEmailProgressCallback
): Promise<{ subject: string; body: string; offerRate: number; datRate: number | null; reasoning: string | null }> {
  const route = `${params.originCity || "?"}, ${params.originState || ""} → ${params.destCity || "?"}, ${params.destState || ""}`;
  const subject = `Load ${params.loadNumber} — ${params.originCity || "?"}, ${params.originState || ""} to ${params.destCity || "?"}, ${params.destState || ""}`;

  // Step 1: Fetch DAT rate for this lane
  const datLabel = `Getting latest rates from DAT for lane ${params.originState || "?"} → ${params.destState || "?"}`;
  onProgress?.({ id: "dat", label: datLabel, status: "running" });

  let datMarketPrice: number | null = null;
  let datMiles: number | null = null;
  let datFuel: number | null = null;
  let datLinehaul: number | null = null;
  try {
    const equipCode = (params.equipmentId ? EQUIPMENT_ID_TO_CODE[params.equipmentId] : null)
      || params.equipmentType || "DRYVAN";
    const datResult = await getDATRate({
      equipment: equipCode,
      originCity: params.originCity || "",
      originState: params.originState || "",
      destCity: params.destCity || "",
      destState: params.destState || "",
    });
    if (datResult) {
      datMarketPrice = datResult.marketPrice;
      datMiles = datResult.miles;
      datFuel = datResult.fuelSurchargeRate;
      datLinehaul = datResult.estimatedTotal;
    }
    onProgress?.({ id: "dat", label: datLabel, status: datMarketPrice ? "done" : "error" });
  } catch {
    onProgress?.({ id: "dat", label: datLabel, status: "error" });
  }

  // Step 2: LLM decides rate + writes email in one call
  onProgress?.({ id: "generate", label: "Generating email", status: "running" });

  const miles = datMiles || params.miles;
  const lastRate = params.carrierLastRate > 0 ? params.carrierLastRate : null;
  const oldDAT = params.carrierLastDATRate && params.carrierLastDATRate > 0 ? params.carrierLastDATRate : null;

  // Hard guardrails
  const maxOfferRate = Math.round(params.shipperRate * 0.90); // 10% min margin
  const minOfferRate = Math.round(params.shipperRate * 0.50); // sanity floor

  // Build pricing data section
  let pricingSection = `PRICING DATA (internal — NEVER reveal any of this in the email):
- Shipper rate (your ceiling — what the client pays us): $${params.shipperRate}
- Your HARD CEILING for carrier offer: $${maxOfferRate} (you MUST offer at or below this — this ensures our minimum 10% profit margin)
- Your floor: $${minOfferRate} (don't go unreasonably low — carrier won't accept)`;

  if (datMarketPrice) {
    pricingSection += `\n- Current market price (linehaul + fuel): $${datMarketPrice} (linehaul $${datLinehaul?.toFixed(0)} + fuel ${datMiles} mi × $${datFuel?.toFixed(2)}/mi)`;
  }
  if (lastRate) {
    pricingSection += `\n- Carrier's last rate on this lane: $${lastRate} (${params.carrierLastShipmentDate})`;
  }
  if (oldDAT) {
    pricingSection += `\n- Market price when carrier last hauled: $${oldDAT}`;
    if (datMarketPrice) {
      const diff = datMarketPrice - oldDAT;
      pricingSection += ` (market has ${diff > 0 ? "INCREASED" : "DECREASED"} $${Math.abs(diff)} since then, ${diff > 0 ? "+" : ""}${((diff / oldDAT) * 100).toFixed(1)}%)`;
    }
  }
  if (miles) {
    pricingSection += `\n- Lane miles: ${miles}`;
  }

  // Build carrier blast history section (learning loop level 1)
  let carrierHistorySection = "";
  const bi = params.blastIntelligence;
  if (bi && bi.carrierHistory.totalBlasts > 0) {
    const ch = bi.carrierHistory;
    carrierHistorySection = `\nYOUR PAST OUTREACH TO THIS CARRIER:`;
    for (const b of ch.blasts.slice(0, 5)) {
      const date = b.created_at ? new Date(b.created_at).toLocaleDateString("en-US") : "unknown";
      carrierHistorySection += `\n- ${date}: Offered $${b.offer_rate} → ${b.status}`;
    }
    carrierHistorySection += `\nSummary: ${ch.totalBlasts} blast(s) total`;
    if (ch.acceptedCount > 0) carrierHistorySection += `, ${ch.acceptedCount} accepted${ch.avgAcceptedRate ? ` (avg accepted rate: $${ch.avgAcceptedRate})` : ""}`;
    if (ch.counteredCount > 0) carrierHistorySection += `, ${ch.counteredCount} countered`;
    if (ch.expiredCount > 0) carrierHistorySection += `, ${ch.expiredCount} expired (no response)`;
    if (ch.declinedCount > 0) carrierHistorySection += `, ${ch.declinedCount} declined`;
    carrierHistorySection += "\nUse this to calibrate your offer — if they ghosted a low offer, go higher. If they accepted a rate, that's your anchor.";
  }

  // Build lane intelligence section (learning loop level 2)
  let laneSection = "";
  if (bi && bi.laneIntelligence.totalNegotiations > 0) {
    const li = bi.laneIntelligence;
    laneSection = `\nLANE INTELLIGENCE (${params.originState} → ${params.destState}):`;
    laneSection += `\n- ${li.totalNegotiations} negotiation(s) on this lane`;
    if (li.acceptedCount > 0) {
      laneSection += `, ${li.acceptedCount} closed deals`;
      if (li.avgAcceptedRate) laneSection += ` (avg closing rate: $${li.avgAcceptedRate})`;
      if (li.avgMarginPct != null) laneSection += `, avg margin: ${(li.avgMarginPct * 100).toFixed(1)}%`;
    }
    laneSection += "\nUse lane patterns to understand what rates work on this corridor.";
  }

  const systemPrompt = `You are a seasoned freight broker at Red Dog Logistics. Your job: decide what rate to offer a carrier, then write the outreach email. You must do BOTH in one response.

${pricingSection}
${carrierHistorySection}
${laneSection}

CARRIER PROFILE:
- Name: ${params.carrierName}
- They have hauled this lane ${params.carrierLoadsCount} time(s) for Red Dog.
${lastRate ? `- Last rate: $${lastRate} (${params.carrierLastShipmentDate})` : "- No prior rate history with this carrier."}
${params.carrierLoadsCount >= 3 ? "- Repeat carrier — reference the relationship." : ""}
${params.carrierLoadsCount === 1 ? "- Hauled once before — mention you'd like to work together again." : ""}

LOAD DETAILS:
- Load #: ${params.loadNumber}
- Route: ${route}
- Miles: ${miles || "TBD"}
- Equipment: ${params.equipmentType || "TBD"}
- Weight: ${params.weightLbs || "TBD"} lbs
- Commodity: ${params.commodity || "General freight"}
${params.pickupDate ? `- Pickup: ${params.pickupDate}` : ""}
${params.deliveryDate ? `- Delivery: ${params.deliveryDate}` : ""}

PRICING STRATEGY:
- You want the LOWEST rate the carrier will realistically accept. Lower = more profit for us.
- But too low = they ignore you or decline. Balance margin vs. acceptance probability.
- If you have past blast data: use it. If they accepted at $X, anchor near there. If they ghosted at $Y, offer above $Y.
- If the market moved up since their last haul, they'll expect more — account for that.
- If the market moved down, you can offer less and justify it.
- Consider the carrier's reliability (score, loads count) — loyal carriers might accept slightly less.
- ALWAYS stay at or below $${maxOfferRate} (hard ceiling). ALWAYS stay above $${minOfferRate} (sanity floor).
- Round your rate to the nearest $10.

EMAIL RULES:
- 3-4 sentences max body. No essays. No corporate jargon.
- Sound like a real broker — confident, casual, sharp. Use contractions.
- Reference the carrier's history naturally (don't list stats).
- If they hauled before, mention their last rate and how your offer compares — conversationally.
- Mention the rate and $/mile.
- If pickup date provided, use M/DD/YYYY format. If not provided, don't mention timing.
- NEVER mention "DAT", "market data", "market rate", "market price", or any data source.
- NEVER reveal margins, internal data, or how you decided the rate.
- Start with just "Hello," on its own line (no carrier name in greeting).
- End with "Regards,\\n${params.senderName}" on its own lines.
- Greeting and sign-off don't count toward sentence limit.

Respond ONLY with this JSON (no markdown, no backticks, no extra text):
{"rate": <number>, "reasoning": "<1-2 sentences: why you chose this rate — internal only, never shown to carrier>", "body": "<the full email body text>"}`;

  let offerRate: number;
  let reasoning: string | null = null;
  let body: string;

  try {
    const result = await openrouterCall({
      model: MODEL,
      maxTokens: 800,
      temperature: 0.3,
      timeoutMs: 20000,
      purpose: "generate_offer_email",
      messages: [
        { role: "system", content: systemPrompt },
        {
          role: "user",
          content: `Decide a rate and write a first-contact offer email to ${params.carrierName} for load ${params.loadNumber} on the ${route} lane. Return JSON only.`,
        },
      ],
    });

    const raw = (result.content || "").trim();
    // Strip markdown code fences if present
    const jsonStr = raw.replace(/^```(?:json)?\s*/i, "").replace(/\s*```$/i, "");
    const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonStr);

    offerRate = Math.round(Number(parsed.rate) / 10) * 10;
    reasoning = parsed.reasoning || null;
    body = parsed.body || "";

    // Guardrail: clamp rate
    const originalRate = offerRate;
    if (offerRate > maxOfferRate) offerRate = maxOfferRate;
    if (offerRate < minOfferRate) offerRate = Math.round(params.shipperRate * 0.82 / 10) * 10;

    // If rate was clamped, fix the dollar amount in email body
    if (offerRate !== originalRate && body) {
      body = body.replace(
        new RegExp(`\\$${originalRate.toLocaleString("en-US")}`, "g"),
        `$${offerRate.toLocaleString("en-US")}`
      );
      // Also fix rate per mile if present
      if (miles) {
        const oldRpm = (originalRate / miles).toFixed(2);
        const newRpm = (offerRate / miles).toFixed(2);
        body = body.replace(new RegExp(`\\$${oldRpm}/mi`, "g"), `$${newRpm}/mi`);
      }
    }

    if (!body || offerRate <= 0) throw new Error("Invalid LLM response");
  } catch (err) {
    // Fallback: use old formula + simple email prompt
    console.warn("[offer-email] LLM pricing failed, using formula fallback:", err);

    if (datMarketPrice && lastRate && oldDAT) {
      const ratio = lastRate / oldDAT;
      offerRate = Math.round((datMarketPrice * ratio * 0.95) / 10) * 10;
    } else if (datMarketPrice && lastRate) {
      offerRate = Math.round(((datMarketPrice * 0.6 + lastRate * 0.4) * 0.95) / 10) * 10;
    } else if (datMarketPrice) {
      offerRate = Math.round((datMarketPrice * 0.95) / 10) * 10;
    } else if (lastRate) {
      offerRate = Math.round((lastRate * 0.95) / 10) * 10;
    } else {
      const rates = calculateRates(params.shipperRate);
      offerRate = Math.round(rates.targetRate * 0.9 / 10) * 10;
    }
    // Clamp fallback too
    if (offerRate > maxOfferRate) offerRate = maxOfferRate;

    const ratePerMile = miles ? `$${(offerRate / miles).toFixed(2)}/mi` : "";

    const fallbackResult = await openrouterCall({
      model: MODEL,
      maxTokens: 512,
      temperature: 0.4,
      timeoutMs: 15000,
      purpose: "generate_offer_email_fallback",
      messages: [
        {
          role: "system",
          content: `You are a seasoned freight broker at Red Dog Logistics writing a FIRST-CONTACT offer email. Be confident, casual, sharp. 3-4 sentences max. Start with "Hello," end with "Regards,\\n${params.senderName}". Mention the rate and $/mile. Reference carrier history naturally. Never mention DAT or market data. ${params.pickupDate ? `Mention pickup date in M/DD/YYYY format.` : "Don't mention pickup timing."} Write ONLY the email body.`,
        },
        {
          role: "user",
          content: `Write offer email to ${params.carrierName} for load ${params.loadNumber}, ${route}. Rate: $${offerRate}${ratePerMile ? ` (${ratePerMile})` : ""}. ${lastRate ? `They last hauled this for $${lastRate} on ${params.carrierLastShipmentDate}.` : ""} ${params.carrierLoadsCount} haul(s) with us.${params.pickupDate ? ` Pickup: ${params.pickupDate}.` : ""}`,
        },
      ],
    });

    body = fallbackResult.content || "";
    reasoning = "Fallback: LLM pricing failed, used formula-based rate";
  }

  onProgress?.({ id: "generate", label: "Generating email", status: "done" });

  return { subject, body, offerRate, datRate: datMarketPrice, reasoning };
}
