FILE: src/lib/agent/safety.ts
================================================
import { logActivity, countRecentEscalations } from "./activity-logger";
import { createServiceClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";

const DANGEROUS_KEYWORDS = [
  "dispute", "lawsuit", "attorney", "legal action",
  "contract termination", "fraud", "subpoena",
];

export interface EscalationCheck {
  shouldEscalate: boolean;
  reason: string;
}

export function checkEscalation(params: {
  confidence?: number;
  emailBody?: string;
  rate?: number;
  ceilingRate?: number;
  roundCount?: number;
  maxRounds?: number;
  allCarriersDeclined?: boolean;
}): EscalationCheck {
  if (params.confidence !== undefined && params.confidence < 0.8)
    return { shouldEscalate: true, reason: `Low confidence: ${(params.confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}%` };

  if (params.rate && params.ceilingRate && params.rate > params.ceilingRate)
    return { shouldEscalate: true, reason: `Rate $${params.rate} exceeds ceiling $${params.ceilingRate}` };

  if (params.roundCount && params.maxRounds && params.roundCount >= params.maxRounds)
    return { shouldEscalate: true, reason: `Max rounds (${params.maxRounds}) reached` };

  if (params.allCarriersDeclined)
    return { shouldEscalate: true, reason: "All carriers declined — no coverage" };

  if (params.emailBody) {
    const lower = params.emailBody.toLowerCase();
    const found = DANGEROUS_KEYWORDS.find((kw) => lower.includes(kw));
    if (found) return { shouldEscalate: true, reason: `Dangerous keyword: "${found}"` };
  }

  return { shouldEscalate: false, reason: "" };
}

export async function escalate(userId: string, loadId: string | null, reason: string): Promise<void> {
  await logActivity(userId, "escalated", reason, { loadId: loadId || undefined });

  // Circuit breaker: 3+ escalations in 1 hour → auto-pause
  const recentCount = await countRecentEscalations(userId, 60);
  if (recentCount >= 3) {
    const supabase = await createServiceClient();
    await supabase.from("agent_settings").update({ enabled: false }).eq("user_id", userId);
    await logActivity(userId, "escalated", "CIRCUIT BREAKER: Agent auto-paused after 3+ escalations in 1 hour", { loadId: loadId || undefined });
  }
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/agent/settings-cache.ts
================================================
/**
 * Per-cycle cache for agent_settings.
 *
 * Reads the row once, caches for 30 seconds (covers a full agent cycle).
 * Every function that needs agent_settings calls getCachedSettings()
 * instead of querying Supabase directly — saves 10-15 DB reads per cycle.
 */

import { createServiceClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";

export interface AgentSettings {
  enabled: boolean;
  draft_mode: boolean;
  test_mode: boolean;
  max_carrier_blast: number;
  max_negotiation_rounds: number;
  rate_ceiling_pct: number;
  auto_book_enabled: boolean;
}

interface CacheEntry {
  settings: AgentSettings;
  expiresAt: number;
}

const cache = new Map<string, CacheEntry>();
const TTL_MS = 30_000; // 30 seconds — covers a full agent cycle

/**
 * Get agent settings from cache or DB. Caches for 30 seconds.
 */
export async function getCachedSettings(userId: string): Promise<AgentSettings | null> {
  const now = Date.now();
  const cached = cache.get(userId);
  if (cached && cached.expiresAt > now) {
    return cached.settings;
  }

  const supabase = await createServiceClient();
  const { data } = await supabase
    .from("agent_settings")
    .select("enabled, draft_mode, test_mode, max_carrier_blast, max_negotiation_rounds, rate_ceiling_pct, auto_book_enabled")
    .eq("user_id", userId)
    .single();

  if (!data) return null;

  const settings: AgentSettings = {
    enabled: data.enabled,
    draft_mode: data.draft_mode,
    test_mode: data.test_mode,
    max_carrier_blast: data.max_carrier_blast,
    max_negotiation_rounds: data.max_negotiation_rounds,
    rate_ceiling_pct: data.rate_ceiling_pct,
    auto_book_enabled: data.auto_book_enabled,
  };

  cache.set(userId, { settings, expiresAt: now + TTL_MS });
  return settings;
}

/**
 * Invalidate cache for a user (call after settings change or emergency stop).
 */
export function invalidateSettingsCache(userId: string): void {
  cache.delete(userId);
}

/**
 * Check if test mode is on (cached).
 */
export async function isTestModeCached(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
  const settings = await getCachedSettings(userId);
  return settings?.test_mode === true;
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/agent/shipper-pipeline.ts
================================================
import { getAccessToken } from "@/lib/graph-client";
import { createOrder } from "@/lib/ttms/client";
import { getTtmsUserId } from "@/lib/ttms/get-ttms-user-id";
import { extractLoadsFromEmail } from "./extract-loads";
import { buildOrderBody } from "./ttms-helpers";
import { sendOrDraft } from "./draft-manager";
import { isTestModeCached } from "./settings-cache";
import { logActivity } from "./activity-logger";
import { escalate } from "./safety";
import { blastCarriersForLoad } from "./carrier-blast";
import { createServiceClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";

interface EmailRow {
  id: string;
  from_address: string;
  from_name: string | null;
  subject: string | null;
  body_html: string | null;
  body_preview: string | null;
  graph_message_id: string | null;
  received_at: string;
  conversation_id?: string | null;
}

interface ProcessOptions {
  /** If true, this email is an awarded quote being converted to an order */
  awardedFromQuote?: boolean;
}

export async function processShipperEmail(
  userId: string,
  email: EmailRow,
  options?: ProcessOptions
): Promise<void> {
  const awardedFromQuote = options?.awardedFromQuote ?? false;
  const supabase = await createServiceClient();

  // If this is an awarded quote, try to get rate from the original quoted_loads record
  let quotedRate: number | undefined;
  if (awardedFromQuote && email.conversation_id) {
    const { data: ql } = await supabase
      .from("quoted_loads")
      .select("lanes")
      .eq("user_id", userId)
      .eq("conversation_id", email.conversation_id)
      .eq("awarded_status", "awarded")
      .order("created_at", { ascending: false })
      .limit(1)
      .maybeSingle();

    if (ql?.lanes && Array.isArray(ql.lanes) && ql.lanes.length > 0) {
      // Use the first lane's our_rate as the shipper rate
      const firstLane = ql.lanes[0] as { our_rate?: number };
      quotedRate = firstLane.our_rate || undefined;
    }
  }

  // 1. Get access token for PDF fetching
  const accessToken = await getAccessToken(userId);

  // 2. Extract loads (non-streaming)
  const { loads, confidence } = await extractLoadsFromEmail({
    emailBody: email.body_html || email.body_preview || "",
    accessToken,
    graphMessageId: email.graph_message_id,
    fromName: email.from_name,
    fromAddress: email.from_address,
    subject: email.subject,
    receivedAt: email.received_at,
  });

  // 3. Check confidence
  if (confidence < 0.8 || loads.length === 0) {
    await escalate(
      userId,
      null,
      `Low extraction confidence (${(confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}%) for email: ${email.subject}`
    );
    return;
  }

  // Check test mode
  const testMode = await isTestModeCached(userId);

  // 4. For each extracted load, create TTMS order (skip in test mode)
  for (const load of loads) {
    // Use quoted rate if available (from awarded quote), otherwise use extracted rate
    if (quotedRate && (!load.shipper_rate || load.shipper_rate === "NA")) {
      load.shipper_rate = quotedRate;
    }

    // 4a. Build order body
    const body = buildOrderBody(load);

    let orderId: number;
    let loadNumber: string;

    if (testMode) {
      // TEST MODE: Do NOT create real TTMS order — simulate it
      orderId = 0;
      loadNumber = `TEST-${Date.now()}`;
      const logPrefix = awardedFromQuote ? "[TEST] [AWARDED] " : "[TEST] ";
      await logActivity(
        userId,
        "created_order",
        `${logPrefix}Would create order from ${awardedFromQuote ? "awarded quote" : "shipper email"}: ${email.subject} (${load.origin_city}, ${load.origin_state} → ${load.dest_city}, ${load.dest_state})`,
        { emailId: email.id, metadata: { test_mode: true, simulated_load: loadNumber, awarded_from_quote: awardedFromQuote } }
      );
    } else {
      // LIVE: Create order in TTMS
      const ttmsUserId = await getTtmsUserId(userId);
      if (ttmsUserId) body.user_id = ttmsUserId;
      const result = await createOrder(body);
      orderId = result.data.id;
      loadNumber = result.data.load_number;
      const logPrefix = awardedFromQuote ? "[AWARDED] " : "";
      await logActivity(
        userId,
        "created_order",
        `${logPrefix}Created ${loadNumber} from ${awardedFromQuote ? "awarded quote" : "shipper email"}: ${email.subject}`,
        { emailId: email.id, loadId: loadNumber, metadata: { awarded_from_quote: awardedFromQuote } }
      );
    }

    // 4c. Send shipper confirmation (via sendOrDraft — test mode handled inside)
    const confirmMsg = awardedFromQuote
      ? `Hi ${email.from_name || ""},\n\nThank you for awarding us the load. Confirming we have:\n${load.origin_city}, ${load.origin_state} → ${load.dest_city}, ${load.dest_state}\n\nOrder ${loadNumber} has been created. We'll have a carrier assigned shortly.\n\nRegards,\nRed Dog Logistics`
      : `Hi ${email.from_name || ""},\n\nConfirming we received your load:\n${load.origin_city}, ${load.origin_state} → ${load.dest_city}, ${load.dest_state}\n\nOrder ${loadNumber} has been created. We'll have a carrier assigned shortly.\n\nRegards,\nRed Dog Logistics`;

    await sendOrDraft(userId, {
      draftType: "shipper_confirmation",
      toAddress: email.from_address,
      toName: email.from_name || undefined,
      subject: `Re: ${email.subject || "Load Confirmation"}`,
      bodyText: confirmMsg,
      replyToMessageId: email.graph_message_id || undefined,
      emailId: email.id,
      loadId: loadNumber,
    });

    // 4d. Trigger carrier blast for this new load (skip if test mode — no real TTMS order to blast for)
    if (!testMode) {
      await blastCarriersForLoad(userId, orderId, loadNumber, load.shipper_rate);
    } else {
      await logActivity(userId, "sent_blast", `[TEST] Would blast carriers for ${loadNumber}`, {
        loadId: loadNumber,
        metadata: { test_mode: true },
      });
    }
  }
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/agent/shipper-status-handler.ts
================================================
/**
 * Handles SHIPPER_STATUS emails: extracts identifiers (load#, PO, ref, cities),
 * resolves orders via a multi-strategy cascade, and drafts a status reply.
 *
 * Lookup cascade:
 * 1. Direct load_number / order ID lookup in TTMS
 * 2. Match sender email → TTMS client → search their orders by reference_numbers
 * 3. Match sender email → TTMS client → filter orders by origin/destination cities
 * 4. Email thread context (conversation_id) — extract identifiers from earlier emails
 * 5. Fallback: reply asking for reference/order number
 */

import { createServiceClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";
import { getOrder, getOrders, type TTMSOrder } from "@/lib/ttms/client";
import { getTtmsPodInfo } from "@/lib/ttms/get-ttms-user-id";
import { findClientIdsByEmail } from "@/lib/ttms/client-lookup";
import { openrouterCall } from "@/lib/ai/openrouter-client";
import { sendOrDraft } from "./draft-manager";

const MODEL = "google/gemini-3-flash-preview";

// ============================================================
// AI Extraction
// ============================================================

interface ExtractedClues {
  loadNumbers: string[];
  references: string[];
  originCity: string | null;
  destCity: string | null;
  originState: string | null;
  destState: string | null;
}

const EXTRACTION_PROMPT = `Extract shipment identifiers from this email. Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown.

Rules:
- load_numbers: numbers after words like "load", "shipment", "order" (e.g. "load 54321" → "54321")
- references: PO numbers, reference numbers, BOL numbers, tracking numbers, any alphanumeric codes the shipper is referencing to identify a shipment (e.g. "PO-123", "FOS11788", "SN3441464", "204000509")
- origin/destination: city and state names if mentioned in context of where the load is going from/to
- If nothing is found for a field, use empty array or null

Return format:
{"load_numbers":[],"references":[],"origin_city":null,"dest_city":null,"origin_state":null,"dest_state":null}`;

async function extractClues(subject: string, bodyText: string): Promise<ExtractedClues> {
  // Always run regex as a baseline (fast, free)
  const regexClues = extractWithRegex(`${subject} ${bodyText}`);

  // AI extraction for PO/ref/cities that regex can't catch
  try {
    const result = await openrouterCall({
      model: MODEL,
      messages: [
        { role: "system", content: EXTRACTION_PROMPT },
        { role: "user", content: `Subject: ${subject}\nBody: ${bodyText.slice(0, 2000)}` },
      ],
      maxTokens: 300,
      temperature: 0,
      timeoutMs: 10000,
      purpose: "extract_status_clues",
    });

    const text = (result.content || "").trim();
    // Strip markdown code fences if present
    const jsonStr = text.replace(/^```json?\s*/i, "").replace(/\s*```$/i, "");
    const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonStr);

    // Merge AI results with regex results (deduplicated)
    const loadNumbers = [...new Set([
      ...regexClues.loadNumbers,
      ...(parsed.load_numbers || []).map(String),
    ])];
    const references = [...new Set([
      ...regexClues.references,
      ...(parsed.references || []).map(String),
    ])];

    return {
      loadNumbers,
      references,
      originCity: parsed.origin_city || null,
      destCity: parsed.dest_city || null,
      originState: parsed.origin_state || null,
      destState: parsed.dest_state || null,
    };
  } catch {
    // AI failed — use regex results only
    return regexClues;
  }
}

function extractWithRegex(text: string): ExtractedClues {
  const clean = text.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, " ").replace(/\s+/g, " ");

  const loadPatterns = [
    /(?:load|shipment|order)\s+number\s*:?\s*#?\s*(\d{3,})/gi,
    /(?:load|shipment|order)\s*#\s*(\d{3,})/gi,
    /(?:load|shipment|order)\s+(\d{4,})/gi,
    /ORD-(\d+)/gi,
  ];

  const refPatterns = [
    /(?:po|p\.o\.?|purchase\s+order)\s*[#:\s-]*([A-Z0-9][\w-]{2,})/gi,
    /(?:ref|reference|bol|bill\s+of\s+lading)\s*[#:\s-]*([A-Z0-9][\w-]{2,})/gi,
    /(?:tracking|confirmation)\s*[#:\s-]*([A-Z0-9][\w-]{3,})/gi,
  ];

  const loadNumbers = new Set<string>();
  for (const p of loadPatterns) {
    let m;
    while ((m = p.exec(clean)) !== null) loadNumbers.add(m[1]);
  }

  const references = new Set<string>();
  for (const p of refPatterns) {
    let m;
    while ((m = p.exec(clean)) !== null) references.add(m[1]);
  }

  return {
    loadNumbers: Array.from(loadNumbers),
    references: Array.from(references),
    originCity: null,
    destCity: null,
    originState: null,
    destState: null,
  };
}

// ============================================================
// Reference number parsing
// ============================================================

/** Parse TTMS reference_numbers field: "3:SN3441464_230PM|9:FOS11788|" → ["SN3441464_230PM", "FOS11788"] */
function parseRefField(refStr: string | null): string[] {
  if (!refStr) return [];
  return refStr
    .split("|")
    .filter(Boolean)
    .map((part) => {
      const idx = part.indexOf(":");
      return idx >= 0 ? part.slice(idx + 1) : part;
    });
}

/** Check if any extracted identifier matches any reference value (case-insensitive substring) */
function matchesReference(order: TTMSOrder, identifiers: string[]): boolean {
  const refs = parseRefField(order.reference_numbers);
  for (const id of identifiers) {
    const lower = id.toLowerCase();
    for (const ref of refs) {
      if (ref.toLowerCase().includes(lower) || lower.includes(ref.toLowerCase())) {
        return true;
      }
    }
    // Also check bol_number
    if (order.bol_number && order.bol_number.toLowerCase().includes(lower)) return true;
  }
  return false;
}

/** Check if an order matches the given cities */
function matchesCities(
  order: TTMSOrder,
  originCity: string | null,
  destCity: string | null
): boolean {
  if (!originCity && !destCity) return false;
  const originMatch = !originCity || order.shipfrom_city?.toLowerCase().includes(originCity.toLowerCase());
  const destMatch = !destCity || order.shipto_city?.toLowerCase().includes(destCity.toLowerCase());
  return (originMatch && destMatch) && (!!originCity || !!destCity);
}

// ============================================================
// Multi-strategy order resolution
// ============================================================

interface ResolvedOrder {
  order: TTMSOrder;
  matchMethod: string;
  queriedIdentifier?: string;
}

/**
 * Resolve TTMS orders for a status inquiry using the multi-strategy cascade.
 * Exported so the draft-status-reply API route can reuse it.
 */
export async function resolveOrdersForStatus(
  senderEmail: string,
  clues: ExtractedClues,
  conversationId?: string | null,
  userId?: string
): Promise<ResolvedOrder[]> {
  const allIdentifiers = [...clues.loadNumbers, ...clues.references];
  // OLD: const ttmsUserId = userId ? await getTtmsUserId(userId) : undefined;
  // NEW: use pod_id for pod-centric filtering
  const podInfo = userId ? await getTtmsPodInfo(userId) : undefined;

  // ── Strategy 1: Direct load_number / order ID lookup ──
  for (const id of clues.loadNumbers.slice(0, 3)) {
    // Try as TTMS order ID
    const numId = parseInt(id, 10);
    if (!isNaN(numId)) {
      try {
        const { data: order } = await getOrder(numId);
        if (order) return [{ order, matchMethod: "order_id", queriedIdentifier: id }];
      } catch { /* not found */ }
    }

    // Try as load_number
    try {
      const results = await getOrders({ load_number: id, per_page: 1, pod_id: podInfo?.podId });
      if (results.data?.length > 0) {
        return [{ order: results.data[0], matchMethod: "load_number", queriedIdentifier: id }];
      }
    } catch { /* not found */ }
  }

  // ── Strategy 1.5: Search reference_numbers for PO/ref numbers ──
  // Fetch 3 pages in parallel to stay under timeout
  if (clues.references.length > 0) {
    try {
      const pages = await Promise.all([
        getOrders({ per_page: 100, page: 1, pod_id: podInfo?.podId }).catch(() => null),
        getOrders({ per_page: 100, page: 2, pod_id: podInfo?.podId }).catch(() => null),
        getOrders({ per_page: 100, page: 3, pod_id: podInfo?.podId }).catch(() => null),
      ]);

      const recentOrders = pages.flatMap((p) => p?.data || []);

      if (recentOrders.length > 0) {
        const refMatch = recentOrders.find((o) => matchesReference(o, clues.references));
        if (refMatch) {
          const matchedId = clues.references.find((id) => matchesReference(refMatch, [id]));
          return [{ order: refMatch, matchMethod: "reference_number_search", queriedIdentifier: matchedId }];
        }
      }
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Reference number search failed:", err);
    }
  }

  // ── Strategy 2: Client email → search their orders ──
  if (senderEmail) {
    try {
      const clientIds = await findClientIdsByEmail(senderEmail);

      if (clientIds.length > 0) {
        // Fetch orders for all matched client IDs in parallel (2 pages per client)
        const clientOrders: TTMSOrder[] = [];
        const fetches = clientIds.slice(0, 3).flatMap((cid) => [
          getOrders({ client_id: cid, per_page: 100, page: 1, pod_id: podInfo?.podId }).catch(() => null),
          getOrders({ client_id: cid, per_page: 100, page: 2, pod_id: podInfo?.podId }).catch(() => null),
        ]);
        const results = await Promise.all(fetches);
        for (const r of results) {
          if (r?.data) clientOrders.push(...r.data);
        }

        if (clientOrders.length > 0) {
          // 2a: Match by reference_numbers
          if (allIdentifiers.length > 0) {
            const refMatch = clientOrders.find((o) => matchesReference(o, allIdentifiers));
            if (refMatch) {
              const matchedId = allIdentifiers.find((id) => matchesReference(refMatch, [id]));
              return [{ order: refMatch, matchMethod: "reference_number", queriedIdentifier: matchedId }];
            }
          }

          // 2b: Match by origin/destination cities
          if (clues.originCity || clues.destCity) {
            const cityMatches = clientOrders.filter((o) =>
              matchesCities(o, clues.originCity, clues.destCity)
            );
            if (cityMatches.length === 1) {
              return [{ order: cityMatches[0], matchMethod: "route_match" }];
            }
            if (cityMatches.length > 1) {
              // Multiple matches — return most recent
              cityMatches.sort((a, b) =>
                new Date(b.created_at).getTime() - new Date(a.created_at).getTime()
              );
              return [{ order: cityMatches[0], matchMethod: "route_match_recent" }];
            }
          }
        }
      }
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Client lookup failed:", err);
    }
  }

  // ── Strategy 3: Email thread context ──
  if (conversationId && userId) {
    try {
      const supabase = await createServiceClient();
      const { data: threadEmails } = await supabase
        .from("emails")
        .select("subject, body_preview")
        .eq("user_id", userId)
        .eq("conversation_id", conversationId)
        .neq("body_preview", null)
        .order("received_at", { ascending: false })
        .limit(5);

      if (threadEmails && threadEmails.length > 0) {
        const threadText = threadEmails
          .map((e) => `${e.subject || ""} ${e.body_preview || ""}`)
          .join(" ");
        const threadClues = extractWithRegex(threadText);
        const threadIdentifiers = [...threadClues.loadNumbers, ...threadClues.references];

        // Try the extracted identifiers from thread
        for (const id of threadClues.loadNumbers.slice(0, 3)) {
          const numId = parseInt(id, 10);
          if (!isNaN(numId)) {
            try {
              const { data: order } = await getOrder(numId);
              if (order) return [{ order, matchMethod: "thread_context", queriedIdentifier: id }];
            } catch { /* not found */ }
          }

          try {
            const results = await getOrders({ load_number: id, per_page: 1, pod_id: podInfo?.podId });
            if (results.data?.length > 0) {
              return [{ order: results.data[0], matchMethod: "thread_context", queriedIdentifier: id }];
            }
          } catch { /* not found */ }
        }

        // Try thread references against client orders (if we have client)
        if (senderEmail && threadIdentifiers.length > 0) {
          try {
            const clientIds = await findClientIdsByEmail(senderEmail);
            for (const cid of clientIds.slice(0, 3)) {
              const result = await getOrders({ client_id: cid, per_page: 50, pod_id: podInfo?.podId });
              const refMatch = result.data.find((o) => matchesReference(o, threadIdentifiers));
              if (refMatch) {
                return [{ order: refMatch, matchMethod: "thread_reference" }];
              }
            }
          } catch { /* skip */ }
        }
      }
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("Thread context lookup failed:", err);
    }
  }

  // ── Strategy 4: Nothing found ──
  return [];
}

// ============================================================
// Status formatting
// ============================================================

// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
export function formatOrderStatus(order: any, queriedId?: string): string {
  const lane = `${order.shipfrom_city || "?"}, ${order.shipfrom_state || ""} → ${order.shipto_city || "?"}, ${order.shipto_state || ""}`;
  const loadLabel = queriedId || order.load_number || order.id;
  const parts = [
    `Load #${loadLabel} (TTMS Order ${order.id}, Load Number ${order.load_number || "N/A"})`,
    `Status: ${order.status || "Unknown"}`,
    `Route: ${lane}`,
  ];

  if (order.carrier_name) parts.push(`Carrier: ${order.carrier_name}`);
  if (order.requested_pickup_date) parts.push(`Scheduled Pickup: ${order.requested_pickup_date}`);
  if (order.actual_pickup_date) parts.push(`Actual Pickup: ${order.actual_pickup_date}`);
  if (order.requested_delivery_date) parts.push(`Scheduled Delivery: ${order.requested_delivery_date}`);
  if (order.actual_delivery_date) parts.push(`Actual Delivery: ${order.actual_delivery_date}`);
  if (order.equipment_type) parts.push(`Equipment: ${order.equipment_type}`);
  if (order.reference_numbers) parts.push(`Reference Numbers: ${order.reference_numbers}`);

  return parts.join("\n");
}

// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
export function formatOrderDisplay(order: any, queriedId?: string): string {
  const lane = `${order.shipfrom_city || "?"}, ${order.shipfrom_state || ""} → ${order.shipto_city || "?"}, ${order.shipto_state || ""}`;
  return `Load #${queriedId || order.load_number || order.id}  ·  ${lane}`;
}

// ============================================================
// System prompt
// ============================================================

const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are drafting an email reply on behalf of a freight broker to a shipper who is asking about the status of their load/shipment.

CRITICAL RULES — DO NOT HALLUCINATE:
- ONLY state facts that are explicitly present in the LOAD STATUS INFORMATION provided
- If a field is missing or says "null"/"Unknown", do NOT mention it or make up a value
- If Carrier is not listed, do NOT say a carrier has been assigned — say we are still working on securing a carrier
- If status is "PENDING", it means the load is NOT yet assigned to a carrier and NOT in transit
- If status is "DISPATCHED" or "IN_TRANSIT", a carrier is assigned and the load is moving
- If status is "DELIVERED", the load has been delivered
- NEVER invent carrier names, ETAs, or details that are not in the data

Your reply should:
- Greet the shipper by first name if available
- State the current status honestly based ONLY on the data provided
- NEVER use internal status words like "PENDING", "DISPATCHED", "IN_TRANSIT" in your reply — these are backend labels, not customer-facing language
- If the load is not yet assigned to a carrier: say we're actively working on coverage and will update them once a carrier is confirmed
- If the load has a carrier and is in transit: mention the carrier and expected delivery date if available
- If delivered: confirm with the actual delivery date
- If no load was found: politely ask the shipper to share the reference number, PO number, or order number so you can look it up
- Be warm, professional, and concise — 3-5 sentences maximum
- Use natural language, contractions are fine

Sign off with "Best regards," on its own line, followed by the sender's name.

Respond ONLY with the reply text. No subject line, no metadata.`;

const FALLBACK_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are drafting a brief, professional email reply on behalf of a freight broker.
The shipper is asking about the status of a shipment, but we could not find the load in our system.
Ask them politely to share the reference number, PO number, or order number so you can look it up.
Keep it to 2-3 sentences. Be warm and helpful.
Sign off with "Best regards," on its own line, followed by the sender's name.
Respond ONLY with the reply text.`;

// ============================================================
// Main handler
// ============================================================

/**
 * Handle a shipper status inquiry email.
 * Returns true if a reply was successfully drafted.
 */
// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
export async function handleShipperStatusInquiry(userId: string, email: any): Promise<boolean> {
  const supabase = await createServiceClient();

  const bodyText = (email.body_html
    ? email.body_html.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, " ").replace(/\s{3,}/g, "\n").trim()
    : email.body_preview || ""
  ).slice(0, 3000);

  // Step 1: Extract identifiers
  const clues = await extractClues(email.subject || "", bodyText);

  // Step 2: Resolve orders via cascade
  const resolved = await resolveOrdersForStatus(
    email.from_address,
    clues,
    email.conversation_id,
    userId
  );

  // Step 3: Build load context for AI
  let loadContext: string;
  let systemPrompt: string;

  if (resolved.length > 0) {
    loadContext = resolved
      .map((r) => formatOrderStatus(r.order, r.queriedIdentifier))
      .join("\n\n");
    systemPrompt = SYSTEM_PROMPT;
  } else {
    loadContext = "No matching load found in the system.";
    systemPrompt = FALLBACK_SYSTEM_PROMPT;
  }

  // Step 4: Get sender name
  const { data: profile } = await supabase
    .from("profiles")
    .select("display_name, name")
    .eq("id", userId)
    .single();
  const senderName = profile?.display_name || profile?.name || "Red Dog Logistics";

  // Step 5: Draft reply
  const userMessage = `Draft a status update reply to this shipper's inquiry. Sign off with the name: ${senderName}

From: ${email.from_name ? `${email.from_name} <${email.from_address}>` : email.from_address}
Subject: ${email.subject || "(no subject)"}
Body: ${bodyText}

LOAD STATUS INFORMATION:
${loadContext}`;

  const result = await openrouterCall({
    model: MODEL,
    messages: [
      { role: "system", content: systemPrompt },
      { role: "user", content: userMessage },
    ],
    maxTokens: 500,
    temperature: 0.4,
    timeoutMs: 20000,
    purpose: "draft_status_reply",
  });

  const draft = result.content || "";
  if (!draft) return false;

  await sendOrDraft(userId, {
    draftType: "shipper_status_reply",
    toAddress: email.from_address,
    toName: email.from_name || undefined,
    subject: `Re: ${email.subject || ""}`,
    bodyText: draft,
    replyToMessageId: email.graph_message_id || undefined,
    emailId: email.id,
  });

  return true;
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/agent/tender-pipeline.ts
================================================
import { getAccessToken } from "@/lib/graph-client";
import { createServiceClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";
import { openrouterCall } from "@/lib/ai/openrouter-client";
import { extractLoadsFromEmail, type ExtractedLoad } from "./extract-loads";
import { sendOrDraft } from "./draft-manager";
import { isTestModeCached } from "./settings-cache";
import { logActivity } from "./activity-logger";
import { escalate } from "./safety";
import { logQuoteEvent } from "@/lib/learning";

const MODEL = "google/gemini-3-flash-preview";

interface EmailRow {
  id: string;
  from_address: string;
  from_name: string | null;
  subject: string | null;
  body_html: string | null;
  body_preview: string | null;
  graph_message_id: string | null;
  received_at: string;
  conversation_id: string | null;
}

interface RateQuoteLine {
  index: number;
  origin: string;
  destination: string;
  shipper_rate: number | null;
  our_rate: number | null;
  equipment_type: string;
  pickup_date: string;
  weight: string;
  reasoning: string;
}

/**
 * Generate AI rate quotes for extracted loads.
 * Reuses the same logic as /api/emails/generate-rate-quote.
 */
async function generateRateQuotes(
  loads: ExtractedLoad[],
  userId: string
): Promise<RateQuoteLine[]> {
  const supabase = await createServiceClient();

  // Get lane boundaries for context
  const { data: boundaries } = await supabase
    .from("lane_boundaries")
    .select("origin_state, dest_state, equipment_type, floor_rate, target_rate, ceiling_rate")
    .eq("tenant_id", userId);

  const boundaryContext =
    boundaries && boundaries.length > 0
      ? boundaries
          .map(
            (b) =>
              `${b.origin_state}→${b.dest_state} (${b.equipment_type || "any"}): floor=$${b.floor_rate}, target=$${b.target_rate}, ceiling=$${b.ceiling_rate}`
          )
          .join("\n")
      : "No lane boundaries configured.";

  const loadsSummary = loads.map((l, i) => ({
    index: i + 1,
    origin: [l.origin_city, l.origin_state].filter(Boolean).join(", ") || "Unknown",
    destination: [l.dest_city, l.dest_state].filter(Boolean).join(", ") || "Unknown",
    shipper_rate: l.shipper_rate && l.shipper_rate !== "NA" ? Number(l.shipper_rate) : null,
    equipment_type: l.equipment_type || "TL",
    pickup_date: l.requested_pickup_date || "NA",
    weight: l.total_weight ? String(l.total_weight) : "NA",
  }));

  const prompt = `You are a freight broker generating competitive rate quotes for a shipper's available loads.

## Your Lane Boundaries (if any)
${boundaryContext}

## Shipper's Loads
${JSON.stringify(loadsSummary, null, 2)}

## Instructions
For each load, generate "our_rate" — the rate we will offer the shipper. Follow these rules:

1. If the shipper provided a rate: Our rate should be the SAME as the shipper's rate or very close (within 2-5% above). We want to win the load. Do NOT undercut significantly — match or slightly beat their rate.
2. If NO shipper rate is provided: Estimate a fair market rate based on the lane (origin→destination), distance, and equipment type.
3. If lane boundaries exist for this origin_state→dest_state: Use the target_rate as a guide, but prioritize matching the shipper's offered rate.
4. Round all rates to the nearest $5 or $10.
5. For each load, provide a brief reasoning (1 sentence) for the rate.

Return a JSON array (no markdown). Each element:
{
  "index": <load number>,
  "our_rate": <number>,
  "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"
}`;

  const rateResult = await openrouterCall({
    model: MODEL,
    temperature: 0.2,
    timeoutMs: 30000,
    purpose: "generate_rate_quotes",
    messages: [
      { role: "system", content: "You are a freight rate analyst. Return valid JSON only." },
      { role: "user", content: prompt },
    ],
  });

  const text: string = rateResult.content || "";

  const jsonMatch = text.match(/\[[\s\S]*\]/);
  let aiRates: { index: number; our_rate: number; reasoning: string }[] = [];
  if (jsonMatch) {
    aiRates = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);
  }

  return loadsSummary.map((load) => {
    const aiRate = aiRates.find((r) => r.index === load.index);
    return {
      index: load.index,
      origin: load.origin,
      destination: load.destination,
      shipper_rate: load.shipper_rate,
      our_rate: aiRate?.our_rate ?? load.shipper_rate,
      equipment_type: load.equipment_type,
      pickup_date: load.pickup_date,
      weight: load.weight,
      reasoning: aiRate?.reasoning || "",
    };
  });
}

/**
 * Build a CSV string from rate quote lines.
 */
function buildCsv(lines: RateQuoteLine[]): string {
  const header = "Lane,Origin,Destination,Equipment,Pickup Date,Weight,Shipper Rate,Our Rate";
  const rows = lines.map(
    (l) =>
      `${l.index},"${l.origin}","${l.destination}","${l.equipment_type}","${l.pickup_date}","${l.weight}",${l.shipper_rate ?? ""},${l.our_rate ?? ""}`
  );
  return [header, ...rows].join("\n");
}

/**
 * Process a TENDER email:
 * 1. Extract loads from the email
 * 2. Generate AI rate quotes for each lane
 * 3. Build a CSV with the rates
 * 4. Send a reply with the CSV attached (via sendOrDraft)
 * 5. Track as a quoted_loads record
 */
export async function processTenderEmail(userId: string, email: EmailRow): Promise<string> {
  const accessToken = await getAccessToken(userId);
  const supabase = await createServiceClient();

  // Duplicate tender detection: check for recent quotes to the same shipper (24h)
  const oneDayAgo = new Date(Date.now() - 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();
  const { data: recentQuotes } = await supabase
    .from("quoted_loads")
    .select("id, lanes, email_subject")
    .eq("user_id", userId)
    .eq("shipper_address", email.from_address)
    .gte("created_at", oneDayAgo)
    .order("created_at", { ascending: false })
    .limit(5);

  // We'll check for duplicates after extraction when we have lane data

  // 1. Extract loads
  const { loads, confidence } = await extractLoadsFromEmail({
    emailBody: email.body_html || email.body_preview || "",
    accessToken,
    graphMessageId: email.graph_message_id,
    fromName: email.from_name,
    fromAddress: email.from_address,
    subject: email.subject,
    receivedAt: email.received_at,
  });

  if (confidence < 0.7 || loads.length === 0) {
    await escalate(
      userId,
      null,
      `Tender extraction low confidence (${(confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}%) for: ${email.subject}`
    );
    return "escalated — low confidence extraction";
  }

  // Check for duplicate lanes with recent quotes to same shipper
  if (recentQuotes && recentQuotes.length > 0) {
    const extractedLanes = loads.map((l) => `${l.origin_state}→${l.dest_state}`).filter(Boolean);
    for (const rq of recentQuotes) {
      const quotedLanes = (rq.lanes as { origin?: string; destination?: string; origin_state?: string; dest_state?: string }[] || [])
        .map((l) => {
          // Try to extract states from quoted lanes
          const oState = l.origin_state || l.origin?.split(", ").pop();
          const dState = l.dest_state || l.destination?.split(", ").pop();
          return oState && dState ? `${oState}→${dState}` : null;
        })
        .filter(Boolean);

      if (quotedLanes.length > 0 && extractedLanes.length > 0) {
        const overlap = extractedLanes.filter((l) => quotedLanes.includes(l)).length;
        const overlapPct = overlap / Math.max(extractedLanes.length, quotedLanes.length);
        if (overlapPct >= 0.7) {
          await escalate(
            userId,
            null,
            `Possible duplicate tender from ${email.from_name || email.from_address}: "${email.subject}" — ${(overlapPct * 100).toFixed(0)}% lane overlap with recent quote "${rq.email_subject}"`
          );
          return "escalated — duplicate tender";
        }
      }
    }
  }

  const testMode = await isTestModeCached(userId);
  const prefix = testMode ? "[TEST] " : "";

  await logActivity(
    userId,
    "extracted_load",
    `${prefix}Extracted ${loads.length} lane(s) from tender: ${email.subject}`,
    { emailId: email.id, metadata: testMode ? { test_mode: true } : undefined }
  );

  // 2. Generate rate quotes
  const rateQuoteLines = await generateRateQuotes(loads, userId);

  // 3. Build CSV
  const csvContent = buildCsv(rateQuoteLines);
  const csvFilename = `rate_quotes_${new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)}.csv`;
  const csvBase64 = Buffer.from(csvContent, "utf-8").toString("base64");

  // 4. Get sender name
  const { data: profile } = await supabase
    .from("profiles")
    .select("display_name, name")
    .eq("id", userId)
    .single();
  const senderName = profile?.display_name || profile?.name || "Red Dog Logistics";
  const shipperName = email.from_name || email.from_address.split("@")[0];

  // 5. Build reply body
  const rateLines = rateQuoteLines
    .map((l) => `  ${l.origin} → ${l.destination}: $${l.our_rate?.toLocaleString()} (${l.equipment_type})`)
    .join("\n");

  const bodyText = `Hello ${shipperName},

Thank you for sharing the available loads. Please find our rate offers for each load listed below.

${rateLines}

We have also attached a CSV with the full rate details for your reference. We look forward to working with you on these lanes.

Please let us know if you have any questions or would like to proceed with any of these loads.

Best regards,
${senderName}`;

  // 6. Send via sendOrDraft — with CSV attachment in metadata
  await sendOrDraft(userId, {
    draftType: "tender_quote",
    toAddress: email.from_address,
    toName: email.from_name || undefined,
    subject: `Re: ${email.subject || "Rate Quote"}`,
    bodyText,
    replyToMessageId: email.graph_message_id || undefined,
    emailId: email.id,
    metadata: {
      laneCount: rateQuoteLines.length,
      rateQuoteLines,
      attachment: {
        name: csvFilename,
        contentType: "text/csv",
        contentBase64: csvBase64,
      },
    },
  });

  // 7. Mark email as "bidding" and create quoted_loads record
  await supabase
    .from("emails")
    .update({ awarded_status: "bidding" })
    .eq("id", email.id)
    .eq("user_id", userId);

  if (email.conversation_id) {
    await supabase.from("quoted_loads").insert({
      user_id: userId,
      email_id: email.id,
      conversation_id: email.conversation_id,
      shipper_address: email.from_address,
      shipper_name: shipperName,
      email_subject: email.subject || null,
      lanes: rateQuoteLines.map((l) => ({
        origin: l.origin,
        destination: l.destination,
        our_rate: l.our_rate,
        shipper_rate: l.shipper_rate,
        equipment_type: l.equipment_type,
      })),
      awarded_status: "bidding",
    });
  }

  await logActivity(
    userId,
    "sent_blast",
    `${prefix}Quoted ${rateQuoteLines.length} lane(s) for tender from ${shipperName}: ${email.subject}`,
    { emailId: email.id, metadata: testMode ? { test_mode: true } : undefined }
  );

  // Log quote event for learning loop — tracks quote→award conversion rates
  if (!testMode) {
    await logQuoteEvent({
      tenantId: userId,
      eventType: "quote_sent",
      shipperEmail: email.from_address,
      shipperName,
      emailId: email.id,
      conversationId: email.conversation_id || null,
      lanes: rateQuoteLines.map((l) => ({
        origin_state: l.origin?.split(", ").pop() || undefined,
        dest_state: l.destination?.split(", ").pop() || undefined,
        our_rate: l.our_rate ?? undefined,
        shipper_rate: l.shipper_rate ?? undefined,
        equipment_type: l.equipment_type,
      })),
    });
  } else {
    const laneSummary = rateQuoteLines.map((l) => `${l.origin}→${l.destination} @ $${l.our_rate}`).join(", ");
    await logActivity(userId, "learning", `Learning captured: Quote sent to ${shipperName} — ${rateQuoteLines.length} lane(s): ${laneSummary}`, {
      emailId: email.id,
      metadata: { test_mode: true, lanes_count: rateQuoteLines.length, shipper: shipperName },
    });
  }

  return `rate quotes drafted — ${rateQuoteLines.length} lane(s)`;
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/agent/ttms-helpers.ts
================================================
import type { ExtractedLoad } from "./extract-loads";

// Copied exactly from add-to-ttms route
export function safeNum(v: unknown, fallback = 0): number {
  if (v === null || v === undefined || v === "NA" || v === "") return fallback;
  const n = Number(v);
  return isNaN(n) ? fallback : n;
}

export function safeStr(v: unknown, fallback = ""): string {
  if (v === null || v === undefined || v === "NA") return fallback;
  return String(v).trim();
}

export function safeDate(v: unknown): string | null {
  if (!v || v === "NA" || v === "") return null;
  const s = String(v).trim();
  const match = s.match(/^(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})/);
  return match ? match[1] : null;
}

/**
 * Builds a TTMS-compatible order body from an ExtractedLoad.
 * Extracted from /api/emails/add-to-ttms/route.ts lines 47-131.
 */
export function buildOrderBody(load: ExtractedLoad): Record<string, unknown> {
  const weight = safeNum(load.total_weight, 1000);

  // Build order_items — always at least one item
  const rawItems =
    Array.isArray(load.order_items) && load.order_items.length > 0
      ? load.order_items.map((item: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
          description: safeStr(item.description, "General Freight"),
          class: safeStr(item.class, "50"),
          pallet: safeStr((item as Record<string, unknown>).pallet),
          unit: safeStr((item as Record<string, unknown>).unit),
          length: safeNum((item as Record<string, unknown>).length),
          width: safeNum((item as Record<string, unknown>).width),
          height: safeNum((item as Record<string, unknown>).height),
          weight: safeNum(item.weight, weight),
        }))
      : [{ description: "General Freight", class: "50", weight }];

  const body: Record<string, unknown> = {
    // Client
    client_id: safeNum(load.client_id, 1),
    bill_type: "",

    // Ship From
    shipfrom_customer_id: safeNum(load.shipfrom_customer_id, 10),
    shipfrom_name: "",
    shipfrom_phone: "",
    shipfrom_email: "",
    shipfrom_contact: "",
    shipfrom_address: "",
    shipfrom_city: safeStr(load.origin_city),
    shipfrom_state: safeStr(load.origin_state),
    shipfrom_zip: "",
    shipfrom_apt_required: load.shipfrom_apt_required === true,
    shipfrom_notes: "",
    shipfrom_shipping_from_hours: "",
    shipfrom_shipping_to_hours: "",

    // Ship To
    shipto_customer_id: safeNum(load.shipto_customer_id, 20),
    shipto_name: "",
    shipto_phone: "",
    shipto_email: "",
    shipto_contact: "",
    shipto_address: "",
    shipto_city: safeStr(load.dest_city),
    shipto_state: safeStr(load.dest_state),
    shipto_zip: "",
    shipto_apt_required: load.shipto_apt_required === true,
    shipto_notes: "",
    shipto_receiving_from_hours: "",
    shipto_receiving_to_hours: "",

    // Order details
    order_type: "NORMAL",
    status: "PENDING",
    equipment_type: safeStr(load.equipment_type, "TL"),
    total_weight: weight,
    total_miles: 0,
    requirements: safeStr(load.requirements),
    notes: safeStr(load.notes),

    // Reference
    bol_number: "",
    reference_numbers: "",

    // Cost
    line_haul_charge: safeNum(load.shipper_rate),
    fuel_charge: 0,
    accessorial_charge: 0,
    total_charge: safeNum(load.shipper_rate),

    // Stops
    order_stops: Array.isArray(load.order_stops)
      ? load.order_stops
          .map((s: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
            stop_type: safeStr(s.type || s.stop_type, "DELIVERY"),
            customer_id: safeNum(s.customer_id),
            name: safeStr(s.location || s.name),
            address: safeStr(s.address),
            city: safeStr(s.city),
            state: safeStr(s.state),
            zip: safeStr(s.zip),
            notes: safeStr(s.notes),
          }))
          .filter((s: { customer_id: number }) => s.customer_id > 0)
      : [],

    order_items: rawItems,
    order_accessorials: Array.isArray(load.order_accessorials) ? load.order_accessorials : [],
  };

  // Dates (only include when present)
  const pickup = safeDate(load.requested_pickup_date);
  const delivery = safeDate(load.requested_delivery_date);
  if (pickup) body.requested_pickup_date = pickup;
  if (delivery) body.requested_delivery_date = delivery;

  return body;
}

FILE: src/lib/ai/classify.ts
================================================
// ============================================================
// TTMS Mailer — AI Email Classifier (OpenRouter)
// ============================================================

import type { ClassificationResult } from "@/types/index";
import { CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT } from "./prompts";
import { openrouterCall } from "./openrouter-client";

const MODEL = "google/gemini-3-flash-preview";
const MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 15;

export interface ClassificationContext {
  carrier_list: string;
  shipper_list: string;
  active_loads_summary: string;
}

export interface EmailInput {
  id: string;
  from: string;
  subject: string;
  body_preview: string;
}

/**
 * Extract JSON from a response that may be wrapped in markdown code fences.
 */
function extractJSON(text: string): string {
  const fenced = text.match(/```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)```/);
  if (fenced) {
    return fenced[1].trim();
  }
  return text.trim();
}

/**
 * Build the filled classification prompt.
 */
function buildPrompt(emails: EmailInput[], context: ClassificationContext): string {
  return CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT
    .replace("{{carrier_list}}", context.carrier_list || "none")
    .replace("{{shipper_list}}", context.shipper_list || "none")
    .replace("{{active_loads_summary}}", context.active_loads_summary || "none")
    .replace("{{emails_json}}", JSON.stringify(emails, null, 2));
}

/**
 * Call OpenRouter chat completions API via the retry-enabled client.
 */
async function callOpenRouter(prompt: string): Promise<string> {
  const result = await openrouterCall({
    model: MODEL,
    temperature: 0.1,
    timeoutMs: 15000,
    purpose: "classify_emails_batch",
    messages: [
      {
        role: "system",
        content: "You are an expert freight brokerage email classifier. Return valid JSON only.",
      },
      { role: "user", content: prompt },
    ],
  });

  if (!result.content) {
    throw new Error("Empty response from OpenRouter");
  }

  return result.content;
}

/**
 * Parse the AI response into ClassificationResult[].
 * Falls back gracefully on parse errors.
 */
function parseClassificationResponse(
  raw: string,
  emailIds: string[]
): ClassificationResult[] {
  try {
    const json = extractJSON(raw);
    const parsed = JSON.parse(json);
    const results: ClassificationResult[] = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : [parsed];

    return results.map((r) => ({
      id: r.id,
      classification: r.classification || "IGNORE",
      detected_intent: r.detected_intent || "unknown",
      confidence_score: typeof r.confidence_score === "number" ? r.confidence_score : 0,
      reasoning: r.reasoning || "",
      extracted_fields: {
        load_id: r.extracted_fields?.load_id ?? null,
        reference_number: r.extracted_fields?.reference_number ?? null,
        carrier_id: r.extracted_fields?.carrier_id ?? null,
        quoted_rate: r.extracted_fields?.quoted_rate ?? null,
        eta: r.extracted_fields?.eta ?? null,
        appointment_time: r.extracted_fields?.appointment_time ?? null,
        origin_city: r.extracted_fields?.origin_city ?? null,
        origin_state: r.extracted_fields?.origin_state ?? null,
        dest_city: r.extracted_fields?.dest_city ?? null,
        dest_state: r.extracted_fields?.dest_state ?? null,
        equipment_type: r.extracted_fields?.equipment_type ?? null,
        pickup_date: r.extracted_fields?.pickup_date ?? null,
      },
    }));
  } catch {
    // Fallback: return IGNORE with low confidence for all emails
    return emailIds.map((id) => ({
      id,
      classification: "IGNORE" as const,
      detected_intent: "unknown" as const,
      confidence_score: 0,
      reasoning: "Classification parse failure — defaulting to IGNORE",
      extracted_fields: {
        load_id: null,
        reference_number: null,
        carrier_id: null,
        quoted_rate: null,
        eta: null,
        appointment_time: null,
        origin_city: null,
        origin_state: null,
        dest_city: null,
        dest_state: null,
        equipment_type: null,
        pickup_date: null,
      },
    }));
  }
}

/**
 * Classify a single email.
 */
export async function classifyEmail(
  email: EmailInput,
  context: ClassificationContext,
): Promise<ClassificationResult> {
  const results = await classifyEmailBatch([email], context);
  return results[0];
}

/**
 * Classify a batch of emails (up to 15).
 */
export async function classifyEmailBatch(
  emails: EmailInput[],
  context: ClassificationContext,
): Promise<ClassificationResult[]> {
  if (emails.length === 0) {
    return [];
  }

  if (emails.length > MAX_BATCH_SIZE) {
    throw new Error(`Batch size ${emails.length} exceeds maximum of ${MAX_BATCH_SIZE}`);
  }

  const prompt = buildPrompt(emails, context);
  const raw = await callOpenRouter(prompt);
  const emailIds = emails.map((e) => e.id);

  return parseClassificationResponse(raw, emailIds);
}

FILE: src/lib/ai/draft.ts

// ============================================================
// TTMS Mailer — AI Reply Drafter (OpenRouter)
// ============================================================

import type { DetectedIntent } from "@/types/index";
import { DRAFT_REPLY_PROMPT } from "./prompts";
import { openrouterCall } from "./openrouter-client";

const MODEL = "google/gemini-3-flash-preview";

export interface DraftReplyParams {
  emailBody: string;
  fromAddress: string;
  subject: string;
  detectedIntent: DetectedIntent;
  agentName: string;
  loadContext?: string;
  carrierNotes?: string;
  rateParams?: {
    floor_rate: number | null;
    target_rate: number | null;
    ceiling_rate: number | null;
  };
}

/**
 * Draft a reply to an inbound email using AI.
 */
export async function draftReply(
  params: DraftReplyParams
): Promise<{ draft: string }> {
  const prompt = DRAFT_REPLY_PROMPT
    .replace(/\{\{agent_name\}\}/g, params.agentName)
    .replace("{{from_address}}", params.fromAddress)
    .replace("{{subject}}", params.subject)
    .replace("{{detected_intent}}", params.detectedIntent)
    .replace("{{email_body}}", params.emailBody)
    .replace("{{load_context}}", params.loadContext || "Not available")
    .replace("{{carrier_notes}}", params.carrierNotes || "None")
    .replace(
      "{{rate_params}}",
      params.rateParams ? JSON.stringify(params.rateParams) : "Not specified"
    );

  const result = await openrouterCall({
    model: MODEL,
    temperature: 0.5,
    timeoutMs: 15000,
    purpose: "draft_reply",
    messages: [
      {
        role: "system",
        content:
          "You are a professional freight brokerage assistant. Draft concise, actionable email replies.",
      },
      { role: "user", content: prompt },
    ],
  });

  if (!result.content) {
    throw new Error("Empty response from OpenRouter");
  }

  return { draft: result.content.trim() };
}

FILE: src/lib/ai/extract-vapi.ts
================================================
// ============================================================
// TTMS Mailer — AI extraction of order/rate from VAPI transcripts
// ============================================================

import { trackLlmCall } from "./track-llm-call";

const OPENROUTER_API_URL = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions";
const MODEL = "google/gemini-3-flash-preview";

export interface VapiExtraction {
  call_id: string;
  order_id: string | null;
  rate: number | null;
  ai_rate: number | null;
  intent: "quoting" | "inquiring" | "negotiating" | "booking" | "transfer" | "unknown";
}

const BATCH_SIZE = 10; // Process calls in smaller chunks for better accuracy

interface VapiCallInput {
  call_id: string;
  caller: string;
  summary: string;
  transcript: string;
}

function extractJSON(text: string): string {
  const fenced = text.match(/```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)```/);
  if (fenced) return fenced[1].trim();
  return text.trim();
}

// /* PREVIOUS PROMPT — replaced to fix rate extraction (was grabbing AI's offer instead of carrier's rate)
// const EXTRACTION_PROMPT = `... The LAST rate or dollar amount discussed in the call ...`
// */
const EXTRACTION_PROMPT = `You are a freight brokerage call analyst. Extract structured data from VAPI phone call transcripts and summaries.

CONTEXT: Our AI agent calls carriers to offer them loads at a specific rate. The carrier may accept, reject, or counter-offer with their own rate. We need to know what the CARRIER wants, not what our AI offered.

IMPORTANT: Read both the "summary" and "transcript" fields carefully. Order numbers and rates are often mentioned in the summary even if the transcript is short or incomplete.

For each call, extract these fields:

1. **order_id** (string or null): The load or order number discussed in the call. Look for patterns like:
   - "order 134068", "order number 134064", "load 12345"
   - Any 5-6 digit number that refers to a load or order
   - Return just the number as a string (e.g. "134068")
   - Return null ONLY if absolutely no order/load number is mentioned anywhere

2. **rate** (number or null): ONLY a dollar amount that the CARRIER (human) explicitly stated as their price/counter-offer.
   CRITICAL RULES:
   - "user", "caller", "customer", "carrier", "driver", "they" = the HUMAN carrier. Their rate goes here.
   - "AI", "assistant", "agent", "system", "we", "representative" = our system. Their rate goes in ai_rate, NOT here.
   - "carrier/user requested $8000" → rate: 8000
   - "carrier accepted $2000" → rate: 2000 (they agreed to this price)
   - If the carrier did NOT state any dollar amount as their price → rate: null
   - If the AI mentioned a rate but the carrier never stated their own price → rate: null
   - If the summary just says "rate of $6000" without specifying the carrier stated it → rate: null (assume AI's rate)
   - "no agreement" / "call ended" without carrier stating a price → rate: null
   - Numeric value only, no $ sign
   Examples:
   - "AI offered $6000, user requested $8000" → rate: 8000, ai_rate: 6000
   - "AI offered $3200, carrier countered at $3500" → rate: 3500, ai_rate: 3200
   - "AI offered $2000, carrier accepted" → rate: 2000, ai_rate: 2000
   - "AI offered $1500, carrier declined" → rate: null, ai_rate: 1500
   - "AI retrieved details with rate of $6000, call ended" → rate: null, ai_rate: 6000
   - "carrier already booked, AI showed rate $6000" → rate: null, ai_rate: 6000
   - IMPORTANT: Extract the EXACT dollar amount. "$600" = 600, NOT 6000. "$1200" = 1200, NOT 12000. Do not add extra zeros.

3. **ai_rate** (number or null): The rate that OUR AI agent offered to the carrier.
   - This is the rate our system quoted (e.g. "I can offer you $1800")
   - Numeric value only, no $ sign
   - Return null if no offer was made by our AI
   - IMPORTANT: Extract the EXACT dollar amount from the transcript. "$600" = 600, NOT 6000. "$1200" = 1200, NOT 12000. Do not add extra zeros.

4. **intent** (string): The overall intent of the call. One of:
   - "quoting" — carrier was offered a rate but gave no response or declined without a counter
   - "inquiring" — asking about a load or requesting info
   - "negotiating" — carrier counter-offered with their own rate
   - "booking" — carrier confirmed or accepted the load
   - "transfer" — caller requested transfer to a human/broker
   - "unknown" — cannot determine

Return a JSON array with one object per input call, in the same order. Every call MUST have a result.
Example: [{"call_id":"abc","order_id":"134068","rate":2500,"ai_rate":2200,"intent":"negotiating"}]

CALLS:
{{calls_json}}`;

/**
 * Extract order IDs and rates from VAPI call transcripts using Gemini.
 * Processes calls in batches of BATCH_SIZE to avoid overwhelming the model.
 */
export async function extractFromVapiCalls(
  calls: VapiCallInput[],
  userId?: string
): Promise<VapiExtraction[]> {
  if (calls.length === 0) return [];

  const apiKey = process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY;
  if (!apiKey) return calls.map((c) => fallback(c.call_id));

  const allResults: VapiExtraction[] = [];

  // Process in batches
  for (let i = 0; i < calls.length; i += BATCH_SIZE) {
    const batch = calls.slice(i, i + BATCH_SIZE);
    const batchResults = await extractBatch(batch, apiKey, userId);
    allResults.push(...batchResults);
  }

  return allResults;
}

async function extractBatch(
  calls: VapiCallInput[],
  apiKey: string,
  userId?: string
): Promise<VapiExtraction[]> {
  // Truncate transcripts to keep prompt manageable
  const trimmed = calls.map((c) => ({
    call_id: c.call_id,
    caller: c.caller,
    summary: (c.summary || "").slice(0, 1000),
    transcript: (c.transcript || "").slice(0, 4000),
  }));

  const prompt = EXTRACTION_PROMPT.replace(
    "{{calls_json}}",
    JSON.stringify(trimmed, null, 2)
  );

  const startMs = Date.now();
  try {
    const response = await fetch(OPENROUTER_API_URL, {
      method: "POST",
      headers: {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
        "HTTP-Referer": process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || "https://localhost:3000",
        "X-Title": "TTMS Mailer",
      },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        model: MODEL,
        temperature: 0.1,
        messages: [
          {
            role: "system",
            content:
              "You are a freight brokerage call analyst. Read the summary and transcript carefully. Extract order numbers and the CARRIER'S rate (what the carrier said they would do it for — NOT our AI's offer). Return valid JSON only.",
          },
          { role: "user", content: prompt },
        ],
      }),
      signal: AbortSignal.timeout(45000),
    });

    if (!response.ok) return calls.map((c) => fallback(c.call_id));

    const data = await response.json();

    if (userId) {
      await trackLlmCall({ userId, model: MODEL, purpose: "vapi_extraction", data, startMs });
    }

    const content = data.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
    if (!content) return calls.map((c) => fallback(c.call_id));

    const json = extractJSON(content);
    const parsed = JSON.parse(json);
    const results: VapiExtraction[] = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : [parsed];

    // Ensure every input call has a result
    return calls.map((c) => {
      const match = results.find((r) => r.call_id === c.call_id);
      return match
        ? {
            call_id: match.call_id,
            order_id: match.order_id || null,
            rate: typeof match.rate === "number" ? match.rate : null,
            ai_rate: typeof match.ai_rate === "number" ? match.ai_rate : null,
            intent: match.intent || "unknown",
          }
        : fallback(c.call_id);
    });
  } catch {
    return calls.map((c) => fallback(c.call_id));
  }
}

function fallback(callId: string): VapiExtraction {
  return { call_id: callId, order_id: null, rate: null, ai_rate: null, intent: "unknown" };
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/ai/flush-llm-usage.ts
================================================
import { createServiceClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";
import { llmUsageBuffer } from "./openrouter-client";

/**
 * Batch-insert all buffered LLM usage rows into Supabase, then clear the buffer.
 * Call once at the end of an agent cycle or API request.
 * Safe to call even if buffer is empty (no-ops).
 *
 * Server-only — do not import from client components.
 */
export async function flushLlmUsage(userId: string, cycleId?: string): Promise<number> {
  if (llmUsageBuffer.length === 0) return 0;

  const rows = llmUsageBuffer.splice(0).map((entry) => ({
    user_id: userId,
    cycle_id: cycleId || null,
    ...entry,
  }));

  try {
    const supabase = await createServiceClient();
    const { error, count } = await supabase.from("llm_usage").insert(rows);
    if (error) {
      console.error("[llm_usage] Batch insert failed:", error.message, error.details, error.hint);
      console.error("[llm_usage] First row sample:", JSON.stringify(rows[0]));
      return 0;
    }
    console.log(`[llm_usage] Flushed ${rows.length} row(s) for user ${userId}${cycleId ? ` cycle ${cycleId}` : ""}`);
    return rows.length;
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("[llm_usage] Flush error:", err);
    return 0;
  }
}

FILE: src/lib/ai/init-llm-tracking.ts

/**
 * Initialize LLM usage tracking for the current request/cycle.
 * Call once at the start of any server-side entry point that uses openrouterCall().
 *
 * This wires up the trackLlmCall function as a callback, avoiding
 * static imports of server-only code in openrouter-client.ts
 * (which would break client component bundles).
 */

import { setLlmUserId, setLlmTracker } from "./openrouter-client";
import { trackLlmCall } from "./track-llm-call";

let _initialized = false;

export function initLlmTracking(userId: string) {
  setLlmUserId(userId);

  // Only register the tracker once per process (module-level singleton)
  if (!_initialized) {
    setLlmTracker(trackLlmCall);
    _initialized = true;
  }
}

FILE: src/lib/ai/llm-pricing.ts
================================================
/**
 * OpenRouter model pricing (per token).
 * Updated 2026-03-24. Falls back to a conservative default for unknown models.
 * Prices from https://openrouter.ai/models
 */

interface ModelPricing {
  promptPer1k: number;
  completionPer1k: number;
}

// Pricing in $ per 1K tokens
const MODEL_PRICING: Record<string, ModelPricing> = {
  // Gemini Flash variants
  "google/gemini-3-flash-preview": { promptPer1k: 0.00010, completionPer1k: 0.00040 },
  "google/gemini-3-flash": { promptPer1k: 0.00010, completionPer1k: 0.00040 },
  "google/gemini-3-flash-preview-20251217": { promptPer1k: 0.00010, completionPer1k: 0.00040 },
  "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview": { promptPer1k: 0.000075, completionPer1k: 0.00030 },
  "google/gemini-2.5-flash-preview": { promptPer1k: 0.00015, completionPer1k: 0.00060 },
  // Gemini Pro
  "google/gemini-3-pro-preview": { promptPer1k: 0.00125, completionPer1k: 0.00500 },
  // Claude
  "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4": { promptPer1k: 0.003, completionPer1k: 0.015 },
  "anthropic/claude-haiku-3.5": { promptPer1k: 0.0008, completionPer1k: 0.004 },
  // GPT
  "openai/gpt-4o-mini": { promptPer1k: 0.00015, completionPer1k: 0.0006 },
  "openai/gpt-4o": { promptPer1k: 0.0025, completionPer1k: 0.01 },
};

// Conservative default for unknown models
const DEFAULT_PRICING: ModelPricing = { promptPer1k: 0.0005, completionPer1k: 0.0015 };

/**
 * Estimate cost from token counts when OpenRouter doesn't return total_cost.
 * Returns cost in dollars.
 */
export function estimateCost(
  model: string,
  promptTokens: number,
  completionTokens: number,
): number {
  // Try exact match, then prefix match for versioned models
  const pricing = MODEL_PRICING[model]
    || Object.entries(MODEL_PRICING).find(([k]) => model.startsWith(k))?.[1]
    || DEFAULT_PRICING;

  return (promptTokens / 1000) * pricing.promptPer1k
    + (completionTokens / 1000) * pricing.completionPer1k;
}



================================================
FILE: src/lib/ai/openrouter-client.ts
================================================
/**
 * OpenRouter API client with retry, exponential backoff, and token/cost tracking.
 *
 * Replaces raw fetch() calls across the codebase. Handles:
 * - Retry with exponential backoff on 429 / 5xx
 * - Token usage + cost extraction from OpenRouter response
 * - Latency measurement
 * - Structured error messages
 * - Direct DB write per call (no module-level buffer — avoids Vercel chunk isolation)
 */

const OPENROUTER_API_URL = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions";
const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
const BASE_DELAY_MS = 1000;
const RETRYABLE_STATUS = new Set([429, 500, 502, 503]);

// Tracking callback — set once per request/cycle via setLlmTracker().
// This avoids importing server-only code (supabase/server) from this file,
// which would break client component bundles that import carrier-scoring → openrouter-client.
let _trackFn: ((opts: { userId: string; model: string; purpose: string; data: unknown; startMs: number }) => Promise<void>) | null = null;
let _currentUserId: string | null = null;

/**
 * Set the user ID for LLM usage tracking. Call this once at the start
 * of an agent cycle or API request before any openrouterCall() invocations.
 */
export function setLlmUserId(userId: string) {
  _currentUserId = userId;
}

/**
 * Register the tracking function. Called once at app startup or request init.
 * This decouples openrouter-client from server-only imports.
 */
export function setLlmTracker(fn: typeof _trackFn) {
  _trackFn = fn;
}

export interface OpenRouterMessage {
  role: "system" | "user" | "assistant";
  content: string | unknown[];
}

export interface OpenRouterParams {
  model: string;
  messages: OpenRouterMessage[];
  maxTokens?: number;
  temperature?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  /** What this call is for — stored in llm_usage for analytics */
  purpose: string;
}

export interface OpenRouterUsage {
  promptTokens: number;
  completionTokens: number;
  totalCost: number;
}

export interface OpenRouterResult {
  content: string;
  usage: OpenRouterUsage;
  latencyMs: number;
  retries: number;
  model: string;
}

export async function openrouterCall(params: OpenRouterParams): Promise<OpenRouterResult> {
  const apiKey = process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY;
  if (!apiKey) throw new Error("OPENROUTER_API_KEY is not set");

  const timeout = params.timeoutMs ?? 30000;
  let lastError: Error | null = null;
  let retries = 0;
  const start = Date.now();

  for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= MAX_RETRIES; attempt++) {
    try {
      const response = await fetch(OPENROUTER_API_URL, {
        method: "POST",
        headers: {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
          "HTTP-Referer": process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || "https://localhost:3000",
          "X-Title": "TTMS Mailer",
        },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          model: params.model,
          messages: params.messages,
          ...(params.maxTokens && { max_tokens: params.maxTokens }),
          ...(params.temperature !== undefined && { temperature: params.temperature }),
        }),
        signal: AbortSignal.timeout(timeout),
      });

      // Don't retry on 4xx (except 429)
      if (!response.ok) {
        const errText = await response.text();

        if (RETRYABLE_STATUS.has(response.status) && attempt < MAX_RETRIES) {
          // Respect Retry-After header if present
          const retryAfter = response.headers.get("Retry-After");
          const delay = retryAfter
            ? parseInt(retryAfter, 10) * 1000
            : BASE_DELAY_MS * Math.pow(2, attempt);

          retries++;
          console.warn(
            `[openrouter] ${params.purpose}: ${response.status}, retrying in ${delay}ms (attempt ${attempt + 1}/${MAX_RETRIES})`
          );
          await sleep(delay);
          continue;
        }

        throw new Error(`OpenRouter ${response.status} [${params.purpose}]: ${errText.slice(0, 200)}`);
      }

      const data = await response.json();
      const content: string = data.choices?.[0]?.message?.content || "";

      // Extract usage — OpenRouter includes this in every response
      const rawUsage = data.usage || {};
      const usage: OpenRouterUsage = {
        promptTokens: rawUsage.prompt_tokens || 0,
        completionTokens: rawUsage.completion_tokens || 0,
        totalCost: rawUsage.total_cost || 0,
      };

      const result: OpenRouterResult = {
        content,
        usage,
        latencyMs: Date.now() - start,
        retries,
        model: data.model || params.model,
      };

      // Write directly to DB via registered tracker callback.
      // No module-level buffer (avoids Vercel chunk isolation).
      // No static import of server-only code (avoids client bundle breakage).
      if (_currentUserId && _trackFn) {
        try {
          await _trackFn({
            userId: _currentUserId,
            model: params.model,
            purpose: params.purpose,
            data,
            startMs: start,
          });
        } catch (trackErr) {
          console.error("[openrouter] LLM tracking failed:", trackErr instanceof Error ? trackErr.message : trackErr);
        }
      }

      return result;
    } catch (err) {
      lastError = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err));

      // Network errors (fetch failures, timeouts) — retry
      if (
        attempt < MAX_RETRIES &&
        (lastError.name === "AbortError" ||
          lastError.name === "TimeoutError" ||
          lastError.message.includes("fetch failed") ||
          lastError.message.includes("ECONNRESET"))
      ) {
        const delay = BASE_DELAY_MS * Math.pow(2, attempt);
        retries++;
        console.warn(
          `[openrouter] ${params.purpose}: ${lastError.message}, retrying in ${delay}ms (attempt ${attempt + 1}/${MAX_RETRIES})`
        );
        await sleep(delay);
        continue;
      }

      throw lastError;
    }
  }

  throw lastError || new Error(`OpenRouter call failed after ${MAX_RETRIES} retries`);
}

function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
  return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
}

// ── Legacy exports (kept for backward compat with flushLlmUsage) ──

export interface LlmUsageEntry {
  model: string;
  purpose: string;
  prompt_tokens: number;
  completion_tokens: number;
  total_cost: number;
  latency_ms: number;
  retries: number;
}

// Buffer is no longer used — each call writes directly via trackLlmCall.
// Kept as empty array so flushLlmUsage() doesn't break (it will just no-op).
export const llmUsageBuffer: LlmUsageEntry[] = [];
